title: Extract shared create/edit sheet scaffold + save/error mixin (case-timeline forms)
description:
The ~12 create/edit bottom sheets each hand-roll the same scaffolding. Affected: weight_entry, marking, condition_entry, journal_entry, quarantine, placement, disposition, exam, follow_up, administration, prescription sheets + aviaries/aviary_form_sheet. Repeated pieces:
- Identical _save() error tail: 'on RepositoryException -> inline _error = errorMessage(l10n,e)' / 'on Object -> reportCaughtError + _error = errorGenericTitle', each with setState(_busy=false). Byte-identical across sheets (~29 copies of the catch block).
- Identical user/org guard: read currentUserProvider, throw RepositoryException('no org for current user') when null (12 files).
- Identical build() shell: guardUnsavedChanges -> Padding(fromLTRB(lg,0,lg,lg+viewInsets)) -> SingleChildScrollView -> Form(onChanged: markDirty) -> Column(min,stretch) with a titleLarge header, the _error block, and a trailing PrimaryButton(actionSave).
- Repeated state fields _formKey/_busy/_error/_isEditing (26 files with the _busy pair).
- Repeated inline _error display block (23 copies) and _pickDate showDatePicker(firstDate: DateTime(2000)...) (11 copies) and _trim helper (6 copies).
design:
Two-part extraction:
1) A SheetScaffold widget owning the padding+scroll+Form+title+error-slot+save-button layout (slots: title, children, isBusy, error, onSave). Composes with existing DiscardGuard/guardUnsavedChanges + showAppSheet + PrimaryButton.
2) A FormSheetState mixin (or small controller) owning _busy/_error and a runSave(Future<void> Function()) that wraps the try/catch tail once, plus a requireUserOrg() helper for the org guard.
Reuse existing DateField/pickDateTime for _pickDate and drop per-file _trim (a shared trimToNull). Migrate sheets incrementally. Note: PocketBase JSVM gotcha does not apply here (Dart side). Higher value but more invasive than federfall-d1et/985j/ko9m; consider doing it after those land. May be worth splitting per-sheet if the diff gets large.
acceptance_criteria: Shared SheetScaffold + save/error mixin exist and are adopted by all ~12 sheets; per-sheet _save catch block, org guard, build shell, _error block and _pickDate no longer duplicated; each migrated sheet's widget tests still green; flutter analyze clean.
close_reason: Already implemented in ff335e7 (extract shared SheetScaffold + FormSheetState mixin), which the commit message states closes this issue. lib/ui/form_sheet.dart added; all 12 sheets migrated; flutter analyze clean.

title: Case detail: collapse ~17 requests into one expanded case fetch (caseBundle)
description:
Opening a case detail fires ~16-18 requests: caseById, animalById, finderById, the canEditCase share lookup, prior-case history, plus 12 separate list requests for the timeline (journal, weights, case_conditions, medications, administrations, markings, placements, dispositions, follow_ups, exams, exam_findings, quarantine). invalidateCaseTimeline (case_timeline.dart) re-fires all 12 on EVERY realtime event.

VERIFIED (2026-07-03, throwaway PB via tests/run.sh harness, as a plain carer with rules enforced): PocketBase back-relation expand fetches all 12 sources + animal + finder in ONE request:

GET /api/collections/cases/records/:id?expand=animal,animal.markings_via_animal,finder,journal_entries_via_case,weights_via_case,case_conditions_via_case,medications_via_case,medication_administrations_via_case,placements_via_case,dispositions_via_case,follow_ups_via_case,exams_via_case,exams_via_case.exam_findings_via_exam,quarantine_records_via_case

- all 12 back-relations resolve; exam findings nest under exams_via_case (flatten client-side)
- view rules enforced: a foreign carer still gets 404; expanded rows honor each collection's viewRule
- rule boundary stays PB rules (no new hook endpoint needed)

Design:
1. caseBundleProvider(caseId): one expanded getOne -> CaseBundle (case + animal + finder + the 12 lists). Needs a repo method that exposes the raw RecordModel expand (PbRepository.getOne maps to T and drops expand) — e.g. CaseBundle.fromRecord in federfall_models.
2. Turn the 12 leaf providers into derivations of the bundle so all tiles/sheets keep working unchanged; move the per-collection sorts client-side (weights measured_at asc, meds -started_at, doses -administered_at, journal -entry_at, follow_ups due_at asc, exams -examined_at, findings created asc, placements -moved_in_at, markings/quarantine as today).
3. invalidateCaseTimeline -> invalidate the bundle: 1 request per realtime event instead of 12.
4. animalById/finderById on the case detail read from the bundle.

Caveats:
- PB caps each expanded back-relation at 1000 records; long cases (years of daily doses) could hit it. Mitigation: if any expanded list has exactly 1000 entries, fall back to that collection's paged forCase fetch.
- weightsForAnimal/examsForAnimal (lifetime views) and casesForAnimal/caseSummaries stay separate — animal-scoped, different screens.
- case_shares_via_case could fold the canEditCase lookup in later (share viewRule may differ — verify first).

Net effect: case open ~17 -> ~4 requests; realtime refresh 12 -> 1.
close_reason: Closed

title: animal_avatar: ref used after unguarded async gaps in the photo flow
description:
File: apps/federfall/lib/features/animals/animal_avatar.dart:104-141

_editPhoto calls ref.read (104-105) and ref.invalidate (137-141) after awaiting a bottom sheet, the image picker, and a network upload, with no mounted check anywhere; on a ConsumerWidget, using ref after the element unmounts throws. The camera flow on Android routinely backgrounds/destroys the activity; on return the code throws "Cannot use ref after the widget was disposed", the upload result is silently dropped, and the avatar providers are never invalidated (stale photo shown).

Fix: capture what is needed before the awaits and add mounted guards, matching the discipline used everywhere else in the codebase.

title: Switching servers reuses the previous server's persisted auth token
description:
Files: apps/federfall/lib/core/pocketbase/pocketbase_provider.dart:34-43, apps/federfall/lib/core/server/server_config_controller.dart:40-49

AuthTokenStorage uses one fixed key (federfall.auth) not scoped to the server URL, and neither setServerUrl nor clearServerUrl deletes it (only explicit signOut does). Failure: expired session -> /login -> "change server" -> configure server B: the rebuilt client seeds AsyncAuthStore with server A's payload, and the PocketBase client attaches any non-empty token as the Authorization header — server A's bearer token (identity claims) is transmitted to a different, potentially untrusted host. The provider docstring at line 25 explicitly promises "a clean auth store".

Fix: purge auth storage on server change, or key the storage by origin.

title: Deep links are lost through the splash/auth gate — cold start always lands on default tab
description:
File: apps/federfall/lib/routing/router.dart:293-343

While serverConfigController/authStatus resolve, _gate redirects any location to /splash without preserving the originally requested location; once auth resolves, /splash is in gateRoutes and bounces to AppRoutes.home. On web (path URLs enabled specifically for shareability) opening a shared link like /cases/abc123 with a restorable session always ends on /cases — the target case never opens. /cases/browse?... query strings are dropped too.

Fix: carry the original URI through the gate (e.g. a `from` query param on /splash and /login) and redirect back once authenticated.

title: Abandoned OAuth flow permanently locks the login screen
description:
File: apps/federfall/lib/features/auth/login_screen.dart:110-133

_signInWithProvider sets _busy = true and awaits repo.signInWithOAuth2(...), which resolves only when PocketBase's realtime OAuth flow completes. If the user closes the external browser without finishing, the future never completes and there is no timeout or cancel path. All controls — including "switch server" and every other sign-in option — stay disabled until the app is killed and restarted.

Fix: add a timeout and/or cancel affordance around the OAuth wait; reset _busy on failure paths.

title: Remove member can never succeed — users deleteRule allows self-delete only
description:
Files: apps/federfall/lib/features/admin/member_management_sheet.dart:146 (and :254), backend/pocketbase/pb_migrations/1700000010_access_rules.js:158

The sheet deletes another user via repo.delete(widget.member.id), but users.deleteRule is "id = @request.auth.id" (self-delete only, never widened by any later migration or hook route) — and the remove button is only rendered when !_isSelf, i.e. exclusively in the forbidden case. Every removal attempt by a supervisor fails with the localized "unauthorized" error; the feature is dead in production. test_rules.py only exercises deletes with the superuser token, so it does not catch this.

Fix: add a supervisor-scoped delete path (rule change or hook route, with last-supervisor + open-caseload guards server-side — see related TOCTOU issue) and extend test_rules.py to cover non-superuser deletes.

Source: flutter-reviewer codebase review 2026-07-02 (features).
close_reason: False positive: users.deleteRule was already supervisor-scoped — the review read the DOWN migration of 1700000010_access_rules.js (line 158). Verified live (supervisor delete → 204). Added non-superuser delete coverage to test_rules.py, which uncovered the real removal bug: deleting a member with only DISPOSED cases failed because PB's active_carer cleanup fired the share-on-handoff hook, creating a share for the vanished user. Hook now skips the share when the old carer no longer exists.

title: pbDate throws on malformed dates; mapper exceptions escape PbRepository._guard untranslated
description:
Files: packages/federfall_models/lib/src/converters.dart:16, packages/federfall_data/lib/src/pb_repository.dart:151-162

pbDate uses DateTime.parse (not tryParse), and _guard only catches TimeoutException and ClientException — fromRecord runs inside op() and its FormatException/TypeErrors are not caught. One record with a garbage date field makes an entire list() call throw a raw FormatException that bypasses the RepositoryException classification the UI error states depend on. All other converters are defensive (tryParse, tolerant coercions); pbDate is the outlier.

Fix: tryParse in pbDate + a last-resort mapping for mapper failures in _guard.

Source: flutter-reviewer codebase review 2026-07-02 (packages).

title: Location picker pairs a new pin with the previous pin's address on geocode failure
description:
File: apps/federfall/lib/features/cases/location/location_picker_screen.dart:116-137

When _resolveCentre() fails (catch at 133) or returns null (127), _address/_city/_region keep the values resolved for the PREVIOUS pin position, no error is surfaced, and _hasLocation stays true — Confirm (229) then returns the new coordinates with the old address/city/region. A case's find location (or release location) is persisted with mismatched geo vs. text address whenever the geocode proxy hiccups mid-drag, with no signal to the carer.

Fix: clear the address (or set an error) on failure/null so "Use" is gated until a fresh resolve succeeds.

title: Exam edit deletes all findings before re-creating them, non-atomically
description:
File: apps/federfall/lib/features/cases/exams/exam_sheet.dart:181-199

The edit path deletes every existing ExamFinding (186-188), then re-creates the new set in a sequential loop (191-199) — separate HTTP calls in an online-only app. A network drop between delete and create permanently loses the exam's clinical findings (the sheet shows an error, but the old rows are already gone). On the create path, a finding-create failure after the exam create (158-164) means retrying the sheet creates a duplicate exam. Contradicts the project's atomicity discipline (intake/handoff go through single-transaction hooks).

Fix: move exam+findings persistence behind a transactional hook route (like /api/federfall/intake), or at minimum create-before-delete with server-side replace.

Source: flutter-reviewer codebase review 2026-07-02 (cases).
close_reason: Fixed: transactional hook route POST /api/federfall/exam persists exam + findings (+ optional exam weight) in one server-side tx; exam_sheet.dart now makes a single saveWithFindings() call. Atomicity covered by test_rules.py [atomic exam route] incl. 'failed edit keeps the previous findings intact'.

title: Disposition edit leaves stale fields from the previous type on the record
description:
File: apps/federfall/lib/features/cases/disposition/disposition_sheet.dart:166-193

The update body only includes release_* when _isRelease, transfer_* when _isTransfer, vet when _isEuthanized, aviary when _isAviary. PocketBase keeps omitted fields on update, so changing released -> transferred keeps the stale release_location/release_type/release_geo. DispositionTile (disposition_tile.dart:45-50) renders release AND transfer fields unconditionally, so a corrected outcome displays leftover data from the wrong outcome type — a wrong clinical/legal record.

Fix: always send the full field set, blanking sections that do not apply ('' / {lon:0,lat:0} clear values, as edit_case_intake_sheet.dart:136-148 already does).

title: Unknown disposition wire value silently maps to DispositionType.died
description:
File: packages/federfall_models/lib/src/models/disposition.dart:41

`type: DispositionType.fromWire(d['type']) ?? DispositionType.died` maps any unrecognized or absent wire value to the most severe outcome. If the server adds a disposition type (or a migration typos one), every such record renders as "died" in the timeline and lifetime views — a factually wrong clinical statement, with no error anywhere. Every other enum maps unknown -> null.

Fix: make the field nullable, add an explicit `unknown` variant, or fail loudly. Also add a test for unknown wire value (see the related test-gaps issue).

title: UX: all text fields are single-line — journal/notes need multiline + sentence capitalization
description: AppTextField (apps/federfall/lib/ui/widgets/app_text_field.dart) does not expose maxLines/minLines, so every TextFormField defaults to one line — including journal entry text (the core documentation field), intake notes, exam notes, medication instructions, and placement comments. Writing multi-sentence care notes in a one-line box with no line breaks is real friction for a documentation-heavy app. Add maxLines/minLines (and keyboardType: multiline where used); prose fields should also set textCapitalization: TextCapitalization.sentences (German keyboard currently starts lowercase).

title: UX: intake wizard has no discard guard — back gesture silently loses the whole intake
description: NewCaseScreen (apps/federfall/lib/features/cases/new_case_screen.dart) is the biggest form in the app (3 steps: animal identity, admission details, photos/notes/finder PII) but has no PopScope/DiscardGuard, while every small bottom sheet mixes in DiscardGuard. A system back gesture or the AppBar back arrow mid-intake discards everything without confirmation — exactly the phone-in-one-hand, bird-in-the-other situation where an accidental back happens. Fix: apply the existing DiscardGuard pattern (or a PopScope keyed on any-field-dirty) to the wizard route.

Back gesture/AppBar back/scrim prompt to confirm while any field is dirty. Tests cover pristine-pops-silently and dirty-prompts (keep-editing vs discard). Commit 364dd95.

title: Security: case_shares can be re-pointed to any case after creation (privilege escalation)
description:
The case_shares update rule (backend/pocketbase/pb_migrations/1700000010_access_rules.js:131) is 'case.active_carer = auth || shared_by = auth || SUP' and nothing makes case/shared_with/org/access immutable. PocketBase evaluates plain field refs in update rules against the STORED record, so a carer can create a share on their own case, then PATCH the share's 'case' field to any other case in the org — granting themselves or an accomplice read/edit on a private case.

Fix: add '@request.body.case:isset = false && @request.body.shared_with:isset = false && @request.body.org:isset = false' to the update rule (new migration), or a hook field-guard like the users one in main.pb.js:295.

Same root cause in weaker form on all case child collections: an edit-share holder can re-point a journal_entries/weights row's case (or org) to a foreign case, injecting records into timelines they cannot read.

Add tests: backend/pocketbase/tests/test_rules.py covers share create/revoke (~line 318) but never PATCHes a share or re-points a child record.
close_reason: Fixed by migration 1700000043_immutable_boundary_relations.js: update rules on case_shares (case/shared_with/org), cases (org), all case child collections (case/org) and exam_findings (exam/org) now reject requests that include the boundary fields (@request.body.X:isset = false). case_shares.access stays mutable. Rule tests cover share re-point, child + grandchild re-point, and legit updates; suite green (113 passed).

title: Rewrite repository layer to use $PocketBase RequestPolicy.cacheAndNetwork
description:
All ~20 repository classes in packages/federfall_data/lib/src/repositories/ need to be reimplemented on top of $PocketBase instead of PbRepository.

Pattern for each repository:
- Keep Repository<T> interface unchanged
- Implement methods using $PocketBase.collection(name).{getOne,getList,create,update,delete}()
- Use requestPolicy: RequestPolicy.cacheAndNetwork (the offline-first default)
- Convert RecordModel → domain model via existing Case.fromRecord / Animal.fromRecord etc.
- Drop PrefsRecordCache usage entirely

Priority order: cases, animals, case_conditions, treatments, weights (the core daily-use data) first; then worklist_entries, findings, exams, and remaining collections.

title: Shared case not visible to recipient until full page reload
description:
Sharing a case with another user does not update the recipient's app live; they must hard-reload the whole web page before the shared case becomes accessible. No realtime push arrives, and closing/reopening the case view doesn't help.

ROOT CAUSE
The live-sync (core/realtime/live_refresh.dart + collection_events.dart) only re-fetches when a PB realtime event fires on a *subscribed* collection, and PB only emits an event for the collection whose record actually changed. Sharing creates a case_shares row — the 'cases' record itself is untouched — so the recipient's subscriptions never fire:
  - cases_screen.dart watches ['cases','animals'] only (not 'case_shares').
  - case_detail_screen.dart calls no liveRefresh at all.
A full reload works because the repo fetches server-first when online and the cases list rule now matches via case_shares_via_case.shared_with.

FIX
Subscribe the case-listing surfaces to 'case_shares' too, so a share/unshare to the current user invalidates and refetches:
  - cases_screen: liveRefresh(['cases','animals','case_shares'], invalidate casesBrowserDataProvider)
  - case_detail_screen: add liveRefresh(['cases','case_shares'], invalidate caseByIdProvider(caseId)) — also covers live revoke/unshare.
  - dashboard_screen: add 'case_shares' to its ['cases','dispositions'] watch for consistency.
PB delivers case_shares events to the recipient because the case_shares view rule allows shared_with = self.
close_reason: Fixed: watch 'case_shares' on cases browser, case-detail live-sync, and dashboard KPIs so a share/revoke refetches live. flutter analyze clean. Pending live two-user verification.

title: Riverpod disposal race in async realtime/connectivity/auth providers

title: FED-3.5 — Stand up a self-hostable instance end-to-end (M1)
description: Validate the walking skeleton on ONE running instance, using the same stack any operator would. Stack is a SINGLE version-pinned PocketBase container: PB serves the API + Admin AND the Flutter web SPA from pb_public with an SPA-fallback route (see pocketbase discussions/4810) — no separate nginx/web container. Image is self-contained: pb_migrations, pb_hooks, and the built Flutter web baked in; automigrate off. Ship changes by rebuild+recreate. All operator config via env vars set DIRECTLY in docker-compose.yml (no shipped .env): allowed origins/CORS, geocoder UA, TZ, APP_URL. TLS is out of scope for the stack — operator brings their own reverse proxy; stack exposes plain HTTP on a host port. Reproducible via 'docker compose up -d'. Plus one mobile debug build for the end-to-end check. NOTE: backups/restore, monitoring, geocoding policy and the full self-host guide are Phase 8, not here. (Was: Caddy + Litestream sidecar — both dropped; stack is now nginx-free single-container.)

title: Extend journal_entries for aviary-scoped free-text logs
description: Allow free-text journal entries (cleaning, feed changes, group observations) written directly against an aviary, not a case (parent federfall-d5co). Decision made: EXTEND the existing journal_entries collection rather than a dedicated one — no field duplication; cost is a dual-parent access rule + XOR constraint + nullable caseId. Ripple is small: nothing dereferences entry.caseId (both sheet and tile take caseId as their own constructor param), so the nullable field only touches the model def, freezed regen, and clinical_mappers_test.dart.
design: Migration: journal_entries.case -> required:false; add optional aviary relation (cascadeDelete). XOR hook on journal_entries create+update: throw unless exactly one of case/aviary is set (PB can't enforce declaratively). Compound access rule: (case set -> existing childView/childEdit on case) OR (aviary set -> aviary-org rule like aviaries: any active member reads, coord/sup edits), guest-safe. Model: caseId -> String? caseId, add String? aviary; regen freezed; fix clinical_mappers_test.dart. Existing case path (journal_entry_sheet sets 'case') unchanged. Repo: add PbJournalRepository.forAviary(id). UI: aviary-variant journal sheet reusing SheetScaffold/FormSheetState; tile reusing TimelineItem/TimelineEntryMenu/confirmAndDelete. l10n de(primary)+en strings + label helpers.

title: Aviary residency ledger (aviary_stays) + centralized animals hook
description: Foundational residency history for flock-care (parent federfall-d5co). current_aviary is today a live pointer mutated in 5 places (disposition after-create/update/delete reconciles in pb_hooks/main.pb.js:160/222/275, merge_animals.pb.js:192, add_animal_sheet.dart:76) with NO history — so 'what happened in this enclosure over time' is unrecoverable once a bird moves on. Add an append-only aviary_stays ledger, maintained by ONE centralized hook on the animals collection so all 5 mutation sites are covered without patching each.
design: New collection aviary_stays: animal (rel, cascadeDelete), aviary (rel), started_at (date), ended_at (date, nullable = current), org. Append-only, mirrors Placement's shape; 'current residency' = latest row per animal (like active_carer derives from Placement). HOOKS on animals collection (NOT dispositions): onRecordAfterCreateSuccess opens a stay when current_aviary set; onRecordAfterUpdateSuccess diffs original().get('current_aviary') vs new — on change close the open stay (set ended_at) and open a new one. Centralizing on animals covers all 5 writers in one place; no recursion (writes only stay rows, never re-saves the animal). Backfill migration seeds one open stay per currently-housed animal (current_aviary != ''). Access rules: aviary-org read (like aviaries), server-side writes only, guest-safe (&& @request.auth.role != 'guest'). Package: AviaryStay freezed model + fromRecord, PbAviaryStaysRepository.forAviary(id). RISK: assumes every current_aviary change flows through a saved animals record (true for all 5 sites today); note in a code comment. Pre-ledger history unrecoverable (backfill = present only).
acceptance_criteria: aviary_stays collection + backfill migration committed; animals create/update hooks maintain stays across all 5 current_aviary writers; access rules guest-safe; AviaryStay model + repo with tests; python rule tests pass against live PB; flutter analyze clean.
close_reason: aviary_stays ledger + centralized animals hook shipped: migration 1700000052, pb_hooks/aviary_stays.pb.js, AviaryStay model+repo, backfill, guest-safe access rules, python rule tests pass live

title: Security: users.updateRule allows a supervisor to move users cross-org (plant supervisor in org B)
description: users.updateRule (1700000011) pins the TARGET's current org (org = @request.auth.org) but nothing constrains the NEW org value; the field guard in main.pb.js only blocks org/role changes by non-supervisors. So a supervisor of org A can create an account they control (create is org-bound), then update it to org=B, role=supervisor — planting a supervisor inside org B (OWASP A01). Moot on a single-org instance (org creation is superuser-only) but schema/hooks/tests all support multi-org. Fix: append a guard to users.updateRule — (@request.body.org:isset = false || @request.body.org = @request.auth.org) — via a new migration using the guest-safe predicate; add a test_rules.py case. Found in 2026-07-03 OWASP review.
acceptance_criteria: A supervisor's update setting org to a different org id is rejected; same-org updates unaffected; rule test covers it; guest sweep still passes.

title: Security: disable/scope Android Auto Backup (protected photo cache leaks to cloud backup)
description: apps/federfall/android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml sets no android:allowBackup / android:dataExtractionRules, so Auto Backup defaults ON. Google cloud backup and device-to-device transfer copy app data in the clear — including the federfallProtectedFiles disk cache (downloaded clinical/intake/animal photos, i.e. the very files PocketBase marks Protected) and shared_preferences. The flutter_secure_storage token is Keystore-bound ciphertext and won't restore usable, but the image cache and prefs do (OWASP A05 / MASVS-STORAGE-2). Fix: android:allowBackup="false", or fullBackupContent + dataExtractionRules excluding the cache dir and prefs. Found in 2026-07-03 OWASP review.
acceptance_criteria: Merged release manifest has backup disabled or rules excluding the protected-file cache and prefs; verified via adb backup / bmgr on a debug build or manifest inspection.
close_reason: Set android:allowBackup="false" on the main AndroidManifest.xml <application> tag. App is online-only with the server as sole source of truth, so nothing is lost by disabling Auto Backup/device transfer; this removes the flutter_cache_manager SQLite catalog + shared_preferences from any backup path.

title: Security: verify PocketBase binary SHA256 in Dockerfile
description: Dockerfile:94 downloads the pinned PocketBase release zip over HTTPS with no checksum/signature verification, despite the stage comment claiming 'fetch + verify the pinned release'. A tampered GitHub release asset or CDN would ship a backdoored server binary into the production image undetected (OWASP A08). Fix: pin the release SHA256 per PB_VERSION/arch and run 'echo "<sha>  /tmp/pb.zip" | sha256sum -c' before unzip. Consider also pinning the Flutter SDK checkout and mock-oauth2-server (dev-only) by commit/digest. Found in 2026-07-03 OWASP review.
acceptance_criteria: Docker build fails if the downloaded pocketbase zip does not match a pinned SHA256; both amd64/arm64 covered.
close_reason: Pinned per-arch SHA256 checksums (from PocketBase v0.39.4's published checksums.txt) in the Dockerfile pbfetch stage and added a sha256sum -c verification step before unzip. Verified: build succeeds with the correct hash, and fails loudly with a corrupted/wrong hash (tested both). Comment instructs updating the checksums when PB_VERSION is bumped.

title: Photo thumbnails serve full-size originals, squished into square tiles
description: PocketBase only generates ?thumb=WxH when the size is whitelisted in the file field's thumbs option — none of the photo fields (cases.intake_photos, journal_entries.attachments, animals.photo) ever configured it, so every 200x200 thumb request returned the full-size original. The client's memCacheWidth+memCacheHeight decode (ResizeImage exact policy, commit 2019217) then stretched that non-square original into the square tile — visible as uncropped, ratio-distorted previews. Fix: migration 1700000049 whitelists 200x200 on all three fields (thumbs generate lazily, covers existing files); CachedFileImage decodes width-only so any non-thumbable source (gif) still keeps its aspect ratio.

title: Add AsyncValue.widgetWhen extension for consistent loading/error UI
description:
Inspired by immich mobile (lib/extensions/asyncvalue_extensions.dart): an extension on AsyncValue<T> that wraps .when() with app-wide defaults:

- default loading widget (centered progress indicator) and default error body, overridable per call site
- skipLoadingOnRefresh: keeps showing stale data while a provider refreshes instead of flashing a spinner
- logs every error it renders (single choke point for surfacing async errors)

Especially valuable now that the app is online-only with no local cache: every screen hits the network, so loading/error states are the common case and should look + behave identically everywhere. Error default should include a retry affordance (ref.invalidate of the failing provider) and use l10n (app_en.arb + app_de.arb).

Sweep existing .when(...) call sites in apps/federfall/lib/features/ to adopt it where the custom handling is just boilerplate.
close_reason: Already largely implemented as the AsyncValueView widget (lib/ui/widgets/async_value_view.dart): default LoadingView/ErrorView with retry + l10n, skipLoadingOnRefresh/Reload, adopted by all 16 async screens. Error logging exists as a better choke point: LoggingProviderObserver.providerDidFail logs every provider failure app-wide (catches even unrendered failures), so the view stays render-only. This session moved the errorMessage(l10n, e) mapping INTO AsyncValueView as the default and removed the identical boilerplate from all 16 call sites (commit a5089e9). The one remaining raw .when() (new_case_screen re-id search) is genuinely custom (inline spinner, intentionally silent error) and was left as-is. Kept the widget form over an extension: const-able, testable, already established.

title: UX: record-outcome action buried in History add-entry sheet — surface it on the Overview actions card
description: The Overview 'Aktionen' card (case_detail_screen.dart _CaseActions) offers ready-for-release, handoff, edit intake, and share — but the terminal outcome (released/died/euthanized/placed-in-aviary) is only reachable via History tab → FAB → Lifecycle group → 'Record outcome' (add_entry_sheet.dart). Closing a case is the most important lifecycle step and sits three taps deep on the other tab from its sibling actions; a user who taps 'ready for release' will look for 'record release' right below it. Add a record-outcome button to the actions card (disabled/hidden once disposed, mirroring the add-entry sheet's isDisposed logic).
close_reason: Closed

title: Logic: quarantine changes never live-update on case detail
description:
case_realtime.dart:14-28 — _caseTimelineCollections lists every timeline source except quarantine_records, yet quarantine rows render in the timeline and drive the active-quarantine banner (case_timeline.dart:119-155). A teammate extending or lifting quarantine — safety-relevant state — does not appear on an open case detail until manual pull-to-refresh.

Fix: add 'quarantine_records' to _caseTimelineCollections.
close_reason: Closed

title: Logic/UX: guests offered as share recipients and carers
description:
PbUsersRepository.activeMembers() (packages/federfall_data/lib/src/repositories/users_repository.dart:16) filters only is_active = true, not role != 'guest'. It feeds the share sheet, handoff and placement pickers. Sharing a case with a guest silently grants nothing (rules exclude guests); handing a case off to a guest makes the case invisible to its own active carer — no feedback anywhere.

Fix: exclude guests from activeMembers() (and consider showing role in pickers).
close_reason: Closed

title: Logic/Privacy: case intake non-atomic — failures strand orphaned animal + finder PII
description:
new_case_screen.dart:261-306 creates the animal, then the finder, then the case, with no cleanup on failure. If the case create fails (offline/validation), the animal and finder remain. The finder is worse than clutter: the finders list rule (1700000010_access_rules.js:119) only grants visibility through a linked case, so the creating carer cannot see or delete the orphaned PII record — invisible (except to coordinators) until the retention cron scrubs it up to 2 years later. Retrying the form creates duplicates.

Fix: create finder/animal after (or with) the case, or delete them on failure.
close_reason: Atomic intake via POST /api/federfall/intake (pb_hooks/intake.pb.js): finder+animal+case+intake weight+quarantine override in one server-side transaction; cases.finder locked via 1700000044 (create+update isset guard, also closes 9hy); client sends one call incl. photos (@jsonPayload multipart). 138 backend + 291 app tests green.

comments.author: Jan-Henrik Bruhn
comments.text: Scope addition (federfall-9hy folded in): make intake a server-side transaction via a custom route (e.g. POST /api/federfall/intake) that creates finder + animal + case atomically. Once clients no longer write cases.finder directly, lock the field down entirely — extend the 1700000043 pattern with '@request.body.finder:isset = false' on cases CREATE and UPDATE rules (a new migration). That closes both the orphaned-PII-on-failure problem and the finder re-point/enumeration hole (9hy), including the update-side re-point that 1700000043 deliberately left open.

title: Logic: case-number generation permanently breaks at 1000 cases/year
description:
main.pb.js:27-43 picks the max case number by sorting -case_number lexicographically. Once '2026-1000' exists, '2026-999' still sorts first, so the hook computes seq 1000 again and every create that year fails on the unique index — no new cases can be opened without manual intervention.

Related in the same block:
- 'case_number ~ {:prefix}' is a CONTAINS match, so a manual number like 'ALT-2026-1' pollutes the lookup (should be startswith or numeric max).
- year comes from UTC admitted_at, so a case admitted just after midnight Jan 1 CET gets the previous year's number.
- concurrent creates race to the same seq; loser gets an unretried 400.

Fix: derive seq numerically (e.g. strip prefix, CAST max) and retry on unique conflict; consider org-local timezone for the year.
close_reason: Fixed in main.pb.js: seq derived numerically in SQL (COALESCE/MAX/CAST over substr), prefix match anchored with LIKE 'YYYY-%' so manual numbers like ALT-2026-1 can't pollute, and a no-op UPDATE on the org row grabs SQLite's write lock before the max is read so concurrent creates serialize instead of racing (unique index stays as final guard). Tests: crosses 1000, foreign-format pollution. UTC-year/multi-org follow-up: federfall-28m.

title: Security/Privacy: protected photo cache not purged on sign-out
description:
ProtectedFileCacheManager (apps/federfall/lib/data/protected_file_cache.dart) serves cache hits without any token check, and signOut (features/profile/profile_screen.dart:14) only clears the auth store — the image cache is never emptied. After logout (or when a different user signs into the same device/browser), previously viewed intake photos and journal attachments still render from disk, undoing the server-side protection from 1700000027_protect_file_fields.js.

Fix: call emptyCache() on sign-out and on account/server switch.
close_reason: Closed

title: Security: guest wall missing on collections created after guest-role migration
description:
1700000033_guest_role.js appends 'role != "guest"' to every rule containing the shared AUTH predicate — but only for collections existing at that point. Migrations 36–42 (quarantine_records, case_quarantine view, admission_reasons, marking_types, medication_routes, animal_species view) copy the old predicate without the guest exclusion (e.g. 1700000036_quarantine_records.js:18, 1700000042_animal_species_view.js:19). Guests can read those code lists and views (species names, per-case quarantine dates), contradicting the 'walled off from all data' invariant. Pattern guarantees regression: every future migration copying the AUTH constant reopens the hole.

Fix: follow-up migration re-running the rewriteRules pass; use the guest-safe predicate in the migration template going forward. Add a guest-access test to test_rules.py.
close_reason: Migration 1700000045_guest_wall_refresh.js re-runs the guest-exclusion rewrite idempotently (normalize then re-apply) across all collections; down reverts only the six post-33 collections. test_rules.py gained a guest sweep (member sees / guest sees none) over admission_reasons, marking_types, medication_routes, quarantine_records, case_quarantine, animal_species. CLAUDE.md + migration comment now mandate the guest-safe predicate.

title: Local medication-due reminder notifications
description: Schedule on-device (local) notifications reminding the active carer when a medication dose is due, built on the existing medication_due view. No server push — PB is push-less by design — so reminders are scheduled locally and reconciled when the app runs.
design:
Source of truth: the medication_due view (1700000024) already computes next_due per active prescription and is scoped to the signed-in carer's cases (MedicationDue.nextDue, active_carer). Only 'scheduled' (with interval) and 'once' produce a next_due; 'as_needed' none — mirror the view, do not invent scheduling here (MedicationFrequencyKind stays the source of timing).

Approach:
- Add flutter_local_notifications (+ timezone for zoned scheduling). OS-scheduled zoned notifications fire even when the app is closed / offline.
- A reconcile step: on app start, worklist refresh, after logging a dose, and after a prescription is created/edited/ended, fetch the carer's medication_due rows and (re)schedule. Use a STABLE notification id per prescription (derive from medication id) so rescheduling replaces the old one; cancel when next_due is null / prescription ended / a dose was just given (next recomputed).
- Notification content: drug + dose + case/animal; tap deep-links to the case (case_detail) or the log-dose action. Reuse worklist_labels formatting.
- Permissions: Android 13+ POST_NOTIFICATIONS + exact-alarm (SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM/USE_EXACT_ALARM) for precise timing; iOS request authorization. A per-user toggle to enable/disable reminders (persist locally; optional org default later).

KNOWN LIMITATION to document: without background fetch / push, prescriptions added or changed by OTHER users (or on other devices) won't schedule until this device's app next opens/refreshes. Optionally add workmanager/background-fetch reconcile later — out of scope for v1. Call this out in-app if needed.

Watch-outs: timezone init (local zone), DST, don't fire for past-due on every launch (fire-immediately vs skip — decide), dedupe across rapid reschedules, respect the app being logged out (cancel all on logout / clear on org switch).
acceptance_criteria: flutter_local_notifications integrated with a permission flow and a per-user enable toggle. A due 'scheduled'/'once' medication produces a local notification at next_due with drug + case; tapping opens the case. Reminders (re)schedule on app start / worklist refresh / dose logged / prescription changed, and cancel when the prescription ends, a dose is given, or the user logs out. Works with the app backgrounded/closed (OS-scheduled). flutter analyze clean + flutter test green. The 'changes by others lag until app open' limitation is documented.
close_reason: Implemented in de8c3a3. flutter_local_notifications 22 + timezone; keepAlive reconciler over the worklist source with realtime listeners; opt-in profile toggle with permission flow (notification + best-effort exact-alarm, inexact fallback); stable per-prescription notification ids; UTC-instant scheduling (no tz plugin needed); tap deep-links to the case incl. terminated-state launch; cancel-all on sign-out/toggle-off; web no-op. Decisions: past-due doses are skipped (worklist owns them, avoids re-firing on every launch); 'changes by others lag until next open' limitation documented in the toggle subtitle and provider docs. analyze clean, 335 tests green, dev APK builds.

comments.author: Jan-Henrik Bruhn
comments.text: Follow-up in ae792d9: dropped exact alarms entirely (SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM removed, always inexactAllowWhileIdle). The chained notification-prompt -> Alarms-&-reminders-settings-page flow was jarring; inexact (~10-15 min window) is clinically equivalent for interval-based doses.

title: Make admission reasons a runtime-editable code list (like conditions)
description: Convert cases.reasons_for_admission from a fixed select/enum into a supervisor-managed, org-scoped code list, mirroring the existing 'conditions' (diagnoses) pattern.
design:
Today admission reasons are a fixed 12-value select on cases (1700000004_cases.js:77), enum AdmissionReason (packages/federfall_models/lib/src/enums.dart), labels in cases_labels.dart (admissionReasonLabel) + ARB. Conditions, by contrast, are runtime-editable: own 'conditions' collection (1700000006), org-scoped, supervisor admin screen (conditions_admin_screen.dart + condition_codelist_sheet.dart), surfaced via _AdminSection.conditions in management_screen.dart; cases reference them via case_conditions rows.

This one is LARGER than conditions because reasons are an inline MULTI-select on the case, not a relation. Plan:
1. New org-scoped 'admission_reasons' collection (label, active, optional is_default to seed the current 12).
2. Migration: convert cases.reasons_for_admission select -> multi-relation (or a case_admission_reasons join) + data step mapping existing wire strings (injury/illness/... ) to seeded rows. Preserve historical case data.
3. Replace AdmissionReason enum usage in new_case_screen, edit_case_intake_sheet, cases_labels, cases_browser filter.
4. New admin screen + _AdminSection.admissionReasons mirroring conditions.

WATCH OUT: statistics group by admission reason (features/statistics/case_report.dart) — that grouping breaks when the value space becomes dynamic; update it to group by the code-list rows.
acceptance_criteria: Supervisor can add/edit/deactivate/delete admission reasons from the admin hub; new-case and edit-intake sheets pick from the live list; existing cases keep their reasons after migration; statistics grouping still works with the dynamic vocabulary; flutter analyze clean + tests green.
notes:
Implemented (code complete, automated gates green; live-PB verification of the migration + access rules still pending).

Backend:
- New migration 1700000039_admission_reasons_codelist.js: creates org-scoped admission_reasons collection (label/active/org) with supervisor-managed access rules (mirrors conditions/AUTH+SUP); seeds the prior 12 reasons (German labels) PER ORG; adds multi-relation cases.admission_reasons; backfills each case from its old reasons_for_admission wire values; drops the old select. Reversible down().
- test_rules.py leak-check updated reasons_for_admission -> admission_reasons.

Models: removed enum AdmissionReason; added AdmissionReason code-list model (freezed); Case.reasonsForAdmission(List<enum>) -> Case.admissionReasons(List<String> ids).

Data: PbAdmissionReasonsRepository (+ active()).

App: admissionReasonsRepositoryProvider; admission_reasons_providers.dart (list + byId); AdmissionReasonsAdminScreen + codelist sheet; _AdminSection.admissionReasons + /admin/admission-reasons route. new_case + edit_intake pickers now read the live active list (edit keeps a deactivated-but-selected reason visible); case_detail + statistics CSV resolve ids->labels via the code list. Dead caseReason* ARB strings removed; new admin l10n added (de+en).

Gates: flutter analyze clean; flutter test 287 pass; models + data dart analyze/test green; migration node --check + py_compile OK.

REMAINING before close: run the stack and verify (a) migration applies + backfills existing cases, (b) supervisor-only write / org-scoped read rules on admission_reasons, (c) down() reverses cleanly. Not pushed.
close_reason: Verified live by user: migration applies + backfills, org-scoped/supervisor-only rules work, runtime-editable admission reasons function end-to-end.

title: Promote quarantine into the case timeline (default 14-day initial quarantine)
description:
Today quarantine is a single `quarantine_until` date field on the `cases` record
(migration `1700000004_cases.js:101`), defaulted to `admitted_at + 14 days` by the
create hook (`pb_hooks/main.pb.js:48-52`), shown as a static Overview detail row
(`case_detail_screen.dart:507`) and edited inline in the intake edit sheet
(`edit_case_intake_sheet.dart:280-298`).

Promote quarantine into the unified case chronology so it behaves like every other
clinical record (weight, condition, medication, journal, marking, placement,
disposition). Quarantine is expected to occur/change often (extensions, early lifts),
so a single mutable field loses that history — a timeline of quarantine events records
*when* and *to what date* quarantine was set or extended.

This mirrors the "initial weight record" pattern: at intake the entered weight becomes
a real `weights` record rather than a field on the case
(`new_case_screen.dart:274-287`). Quarantine should get the same treatment — a default
**initial 14-day quarantine** created as a timeline record at case creation.

## Goal

- New quarantine timeline kind following the established pattern (see CLAUDE.md "Case
  timeline pattern"): a collection + model + repo + `@riverpod` provider + `showXSheet()`
  bottom sheet (create/edit) + a `TimelineItem` tile + a sealed `_Event` subclass in
  `case_timeline.dart`.
- Each record carries the quarantine end date (`quarantine_until`) and an event timestamp
  (when it was set/extended); an optional reason/note. Multiple records = extensions /
  re-quarantine; the latest record's end date is the current quarantine status.
- Default initial quarantine of **14 days** from admission, created automatically — like
  the initial weight record.

## Open decision (resolve before implementing)

The worklist and dashboard currently sort/surface cases by `case.quarantine_until`
(`worklist.dart`, `dashboard_providers.dart`). Two options:

1. **Keep a denormalized `quarantine_until` mirror on the case**, maintained by a hook
   to always equal the latest quarantine record (parallels how case `status` /
   animal `lifetime_status` are hook-owned). Cheapest for worklist/dashboard; no extra
   joins. **(recommended)**
2. **Drop the field entirely** and have worklist/dashboard query the quarantine records.
   Cleaner data model but more query work and N+1 risk on list views.

Decide which before touching the backend; option 1 keeps worklist/dashboard untouched.

## Where the default is created

Match the initial-weight approach: prefer creating the default quarantine record in the
backend create hook (`main.pb.js`) so it exists regardless of client, OR in
`new_case_screen.dart` alongside the intake weight. Note `main.pb.js` already owns the
14-day quarantine default — repoint it to create a record instead of (or in addition to,
if option 1) setting the field.

## Likely touch points

Backend:
- New migration: `quarantine_records` collection (relation `case` cascade, `quarantine_until`
  date required, `reason` text optional, `set_by` user, `org`, timestamps) + access rules
  (`1700000010_access_rules.js`, org-scoped).
- `main.pb.js`: create the default 14-day quarantine record on case create; (option 1) keep
  `quarantine_until` mirror in sync.
- Rule/hook tests (`tests/test_rules.py`).

Models / data:
- `federfall_models`: add `Quarantine` model + `fromRecord` mapper (see `Disposition`).
- `federfall_data`: `QuarantineRecordsRepository` + provider wiring in
  `repository_providers.dart`.

App:
- `features/cases/quarantine/`: providers, `quarantine_sheet.dart`, `quarantine_tile.dart`.
- `case_timeline.dart`: `_QuarantineEvent` subclass + fetch in build.
- `add_entry_sheet.dart`: add quarantine to the "add" picker.
- `case_detail_screen.dart`: drop the static Overview quarantine row (now on History).
- `edit_case_intake_sheet.dart`: remove the inline quarantine field.
- l10n: `app_de.arb` + `app_en.arb` strings (German primary).

Don't forget codegen (`build_runner` for freezed/riverpod, `flutter gen-l10n`) and the
quality gates (`flutter analyze` + tests; package `dart analyze`/`dart test`).
design:
DECISION (resolved): use a PocketBase VIEW collection for the worklist/dashboard quarantine end date — NOT a denormalized mirror field and NOT a client-side join. This is the established pattern here (see medication_due_view 1700000024 — same shape: compute a derived date per record so the worklist gets it in one query). 'Current quarantine end per case' = MAX(quarantine_until) over the case's quarantine_records (or latest by event timestamp if early-lift records are allowed — decide alongside lift semantics).

Two shapes: (1) dedicated view 'quarantine_current' (id=case, quarantine_until=MAX(...)), org-scoped like medication_due; or (2) cheaper — add a computed quarantine_until column to the existing case_summaries view so current readers get it for free.

Realtime caveat is already solved: views emit no realtime, so add 'quarantine_records' to worklistLiveCollections (worklist_providers.dart) — the base-collection watch + 1-minute worklistTicker already handle the clock-driven 'quarantine ending' transition (the ticker comment literally names it). This means NO mirror field and NO sync hook: the only hook work is creating the default 14-day quarantine record at case create. Supersedes the option-1/option-2 'Open decision' in the description.
acceptance_criteria: Quarantine appears as a timeline kind in the case History (provider + showXSheet + TimelineItem tile + _Event subclass); a default 14-day quarantine record is created automatically at case creation (like the initial weight record); the static Overview quarantine row and the inline edit-sheet field are removed; worklist/dashboard quarantine sorting still works; flutter analyze clean + tests green; backend migration + hook + rule tests in place.
close_reason: Implemented across 3 commits on main (3f4b0aa, 7544538, dd0b25e): quarantine_records collection + case_quarantine view + backfill/drop-column migrations + org-aware default hook; Quarantine/CaseQuarantine models, repos, providers; timeline sheet/tile with started+ended entries and inline 'end now'; org-wide default setting + per-case override at intake; worklist+dashboard read the view; l10n de/en. Gates green (analyze clean; 287 app + 47 models + 69 data tests). Migrations applied to dev stack. In-app behavioural spot-check left to the user.

comments.author: Jan-Henrik Bruhn
comments.text: Implemented (commit 3f4b0aa). Backend: quarantine_records collection + case_quarantine view + backfill/drop-column migrations + after-create hook for the default 14-day record. App: model/repo/providers/sheet/tile/timeline wiring; worklist+dashboard read the view; Overview row + inline edit field removed; l10n de/en. Gates green (analyze clean; 285 app + 47 models + 69 data tests). Migrations applied to the running dev stack (collections resolve via API). REMAINING manual verification (needs app login / admin creds I don't have here): create a case and confirm the default 14-day quarantine record appears on the timeline, and that extending/lifting updates the worklist+dashboard via the view.

title: Add background sync via WorkManager (iOS/Android) — push writes when app is backgrounded
description:
pocketbase_drift's sync retry only fires when the app is active and connectivity returns. For wildlife rehabilitators who log a medication and immediately put the phone in their pocket, the write queues locally but only syncs when they re-open the app.

- Evaluate adding workmanager (^3.0.0) to trigger sync on backgrounded iOS/Android
- On connectivity RESTORED event, schedule a WorkManager one-off task that calls _retrySyncForAllServices()
- On web: use ServiceWorker background sync API (navigator.serviceWorker.ready.then(...))
- Add retryLocal() progress reporting to the UI (sync indicator in app bar)
- Test: kill app immediately after creating a case offline → app restart should sync it

title: Handle logout — set clearOnLogout: false on $AuthStore; verify schema persistence
description:
pocketbase_drift's $AuthStore.clear() wipes the entire local Drift DB by default. For Federfall, the cache should be preserved on logout (carers may share a device), only cleared on explicit app reset.

- Set clearOnLogout: false on $AuthStore
- Verify schema (pb_schema.json) is NOT cleared on logout
- Verify case/case_condition/treatment data IS cleared on logout
- Test: log in → create a case offline → log out → log back in → case should be gone
- Remove SecureAuthTokenStorage / PrefsAuthTokenStorage once $AuthStore.prefs is wired

title: Adapt LiveRefresh / case_realtime.dart for pocketbase_drift's realtime bridge
description:
pocketbase_drift pipes PocketBase realtime events into the local Drift DB (onEvent() → $create / $update / $delete on local SQLite). Federfall's existing ServiceMixin, LiveRefresh and case_realtime.dart wiring should be checked for compatibility.

- Verify ServiceMixin's watchRecords() stream still fires and triggers UI rebuilds
- Verify onEvent() writes to Drift do not interfere with existing stream logic
- Adapt caseRealtime to use $PocketBase.realtime if needed
- Test: create/update/delete a case from Admin UI → verify Flutter app updates within 5s

Note: the realtime service is currently delegated to the base PocketBase SDK class (not overridden in $PocketBase).

title: Set up WASM SQLite for web (sqlite3.wasm + drift_worker.dart.js)
description:
pocketbase_drift uses Drift with SQLite. On web this requires WASM files served from the web/ directory.

- Download sqlite3.wasm from the sqlite3.dart releases
- Download drift_worker.dart.js from the drift package
- Place both in apps/federfall/web/
- Verify web build succeeds (flutter build web)
- Test offline reads/writes in browser (Chrome DevTools → Network → Offline)

Currently Federfall uses SharedPreferences (localStorage) for web cache — this replaces it with a proper SQLite store.

title: Migrate to pocketbase_drift — offline-first sync engine
description:
Replace the current read-through cache (PrefsRecordCache) with pocketbase_drift (^0.4.3), a proper offline-first SQLite-backed layer for PocketBase.

CURRENT BEHAVIOUR (broken):
- All writes (create/update/delete) throw RepositoryException.network immediately when offline
- Cache is bounded: 100 records / 20 lists per collection, no TTL, FIFO eviction
- offlineBanner shows "Offline – showing saved data" — misleading, because no writes are possible
- No background sync; writes never survive app restart

DESIRED BEHAVIOUR:
- Writes queue locally in SQLite when offline; sync automatically on reconnect
- Reads served from cache instantly; network updates in background
- Conflict resolution: 5 retries then silent discard (acceptable for wildlife rehab use case)
- Schema-bundled validation for local writes

See: https://pub.dev/packages/pocketbase_drift
See: internal code review — pocketbase_drift gap analysis

title: Add OAuth2 provisioning integration tests (oauth2_provisioning.pb.js)
description:
The Python integration suite (backend/pocketbase/tests/test_rules.py) covers access rules, hooks, and edge cases, but there is no automated test for the OAuth2 provisioning flow (pb_hooks/oauth2_provisioning.pb.js) — particularly group-to-role mapping and the FEDERFALL_OIDC_ALLOWED_GROUPS gate.

Recommendation: add OAuth2 provisioning tests covering:

- group-based role assignment

Note: rule/hook tests need a live PocketBase (run via run.sh), not the Flutter test suite.
Source: code review finding #6 (Testing).

title: Sheets/dialogs lack PopScope for unsaved-changes / back-nav
description:
Sheets and dialogs don't use PopScope (or PopEntry) to control back navigation on Android. An accidental swipe or back gesture can dismiss a form with unsaved changes without confirmation.

Recommendation: wrap sheet/dialog content in PopScope to intercept dismissal when the form is dirty and prompt to confirm discarding unsaved changes.

Files: sheet/dialog widgets — add_entry_sheet.dart, edit_animal_sheet.dart, and the other showXSheet() bottom sheets.
Source: code review finding #2.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Jan-Henrik Bruhn
comments.text:
Implemented a reusable DiscardGuard (lib/ui/discard_guard.dart, exported via ui.dart):
- mixin DiscardGuard on a sheet State -> markDirty()/isDirty/guardUnsavedChanges(child:)
- confirmDiscardChanges() AlertDialog with new l10n keys (discardChangesTitle/Message/KeepEditing/Confirm, EN+DE)
- Wiring per sheet: 'with DiscardGuard', Form(onChanged: markDirty) for text/FormField widgets, markDirty() in non-form handlers (date pickers, dropdowns that aren't FormFields, switches, staged photos), and build body wrapped in guardUnsavedChanges.

Applied to all 18 create/edit form sheets (weights, journal, exam, conditions, markings, placements, follow-ups, administration, prescription, disposition, edit_case_intake, add/edit_animal, aviary_form, edit_profile, invite_member, condition_codelist, member_management). Skipped non-form/action sheets (add_entry chooser, case_share grant-action, cases_screen live filters).

SCOPE/LIMITATION (decided with user): guards the Android back button/gesture + scrim tap (both route through Navigator.maybePop, which honours PopScope). Swipe-down drag-to-dismiss calls Navigator.pop() directly (bottom_sheet.dart onClosing) and bypasses PopScope -> intentionally NOT guarded, to avoid disabling the drag affordance app-wide. See finding #2.

flutter analyze clean; flutter test green (294 passing).

title: Case detail pane shows "not found" in wide view when the user has no cases
description: On wide/expanded screens (two-pane list+detail layout, federfall-zbe), the case detail pane appears to show a 'not found' state instead of the empty/select-a-case placeholder when the signed-in user has no cases yet (e.g. a fresh account). Expected: the right pane should show the DetailPanePlaceholder ('select a case' / empty hint), not a not-found error. Reported by user (suspected repro: brand-new user with zero cases). Investigate the cases ShellRoute on expanded width: section-root should render DetailPanePlaceholder when nothing is selected; check whether an empty list / no-selection path is routing into ':id' detail or NotFoundScreen. Likely in lib/routing/router.dart (cases branch) + the cases two-pane wiring; see adaptive-two-pane-layout memory.
close_reason: Not reproducible. Traced the suspected repro (fresh user, zero cases, expanded/wide width): the cases ShellRoute section-root renders DetailPanePlaceholder, not a detail. The 'Not found' string comes only from CaseDetailScreen 404ing caseByIdProvider, which requires a real /cases/:id match — nothing auto-navigates there for an empty list (no auto-select, no route persistence). A repro test at 1400px width with an empty cases repo showed the placeholder, not not-found. 'Not found' only legitimately appears when navigating to a specific stale/invalid case id (bookmarked URL, or a case deleted/unshared live), which is correct behavior.

title: OAuth2 self-service provisioning: guest role + group gating + first-user supervisor
description: Make OAuth2 usable as a sign-in method that can also self-provision accounts, on top of federfall-uvf (which enabled OAuth2 + env provider config). Decided shape: HYBRID. (1) Add a 'guest' role; new self-registered OAuth2 users default to guest. (2) Guest-proof the access rules: a guest is is_active=true (can log in) but the access boundary excludes role='guest' everywhere, so they see/do nothing until promoted — show an 'awaiting access' state. (3) pb_hooks OAuth2 provisioning (onRecordAuthWithOAuth2Request, isNewRecord): read IdP groups from oAuth2User.rawUser; optional group->role mapping (env), optional allow-list gating (env); set role/org/is_active on the new record (also fixes the role-required 400 that blocks OAuth2 signup today). (4) Chicken-and-egg: the FIRST user (when no active supervisor exists) OR a configured supervisor group becomes supervisor. (5) Optional env toggle to disable password auth so OAuth2 can be the ONLY method (info endpoint already reports auth.password=false; login screen already hides the password form). NOTE: the OAuth2 login-screen buttons + awaiting-access UI ride on federfall-pj3; the provisioning hook only fires on a real OAuth2 flow so end-to-end needs a live IdP — guest-rule enforcement is testable via the API + rule tests.
notes:
Backend DONE + tested: guest role + access-rule wall-off (migration 1700000033; 6 new rule tests, suite now 98/0), OAuth2 self-provisioning hook (oauth2_provisioning.pb.js: first-user/admin-group->supervisor, group->role mapping, allowed-groups gating, sets role/org/is_active), and FEDERFALL_PASSWORD_AUTH toggle for OAuth2-only (info reports password:false). Documented in compose/DEPLOYMENT/README. CAVEAT: the provisioning hook loads cleanly and is wired to the confirmed onRecordAuthWithOAuth2Request API, but its group-mapping/role-assignment path only fires on a real OAuth2 flow — needs live-IdP verification (no mock IdP in the test rig). App UI (provider buttons, guest awaiting-access screen, redirect handling) is federfall-pj3.
VERIFIED end-to-end against the mock IdP (navikt/mock-oauth2-server): drove the full auth-code flow (authorize -> code -> /api/collections/users/auth-with-oauth2 with PKCE) — admin-group user provisions as supervisor, no-group user as guest, both with org + is_active set and signed in. Fixed 4 bugs found in testing (see commit febb4c2): createRule (oauth2 context), hook now uses e.createData not e.record, createData object assign, dropped protected verified field. Rule tests 99/0 incl. anonymous-create-denied. Backend complete.
close_reason: Closed

title: Responsive/adaptive layouts for web & large screens (tablet)
description:
The app is built mobile-first and works well on phones, but on the web and larger screens (tablet, desktop browser) it just stretches the single-pane phone UI — navigating into a list item opens a whole new full-screen 'window' where a wide screen has room to show list AND detail together. Make the layout adaptive so large screens use their space.

Concrete opportunities:
- List-detail (two-pane): cases list, animals registry, aviaries — show the list on the left and the selected item's detail on the right on wide screens, instead of pushing a new route. Falls back to the current full-screen navigation on narrow screens.
- Case detail: on wide screens show Overview and History side-by-side simultaneously, rather than behind the Übersicht/Verlauf tabs. Keep the tabs on narrow screens.
- App chrome: NavigationRail (or extended rail) on wide screens vs the bottom NavigationBar on mobile (the nav shell).

Approach — derive proper UX concepts first, then implement the Flutter way, NO code duplication:
- Define breakpoints once (compact / medium / expanded, per Material 3 window-size classes) and a single place that maps them to layout decisions; don't sprinkle MediaQuery checks.
- Reuse the EXISTING composable widgets (CaseTimeline, the Overview sections, list tiles, DetailHeader, etc.) in both the single-pane and two-pane arrangements — the panes are just different containers around the same widgets, not reimplementations.
- Use Material 3 canonical layouts / adaptive scaffolding (LayoutBuilder + the official adaptive patterns, e.g. flutter_adaptive_scaffold or an equivalent in-house AdaptiveScaffold) and NavigationRail<->NavigationBar.
- Mind go_router: the selected list item / detail must stay URL-addressable (deep links, browser back) — likely a StatefulShellRoute or a selected-id query/path that drives the detail pane; the two-pane and pushed-route views should share one source of selection state.
- Keep it accessible and not regress the phone experience.

This is sizeable and design-led — start by producing the UX concept (breakpoints, which surfaces get two-pane, how selection/routing works, mockups), then split implementation per surface (it may warrant promotion to an epic with child issues once the concept is agreed).
acceptance_criteria: A documented adaptive-layout concept exists (breakpoints, per-surface behaviour, routing/selection model). On expanded widths: the cases/animals/aviaries lists show list+detail side-by-side and the case detail shows Overview+History together; on compact widths the current phone UX is unchanged. Layout decisions come from one shared breakpoint source; the same content widgets are reused across panes with no duplication; deep links and browser back still work; flutter analyze clean + tests green.
close_reason: Closed

title: Gate all write-operation UI behind the user's permissions
description:
Across the app, hide (or disable) every write/mutating UI control when the current user is not allowed to perform it, so the UI matches the server access rules instead of offering actions that fail on save. The backend access rules in 1700000010_access_rules.js are the real boundary; this is about not surfacing dead-end controls (esp. to read-only shared viewers and lower-privileged roles).

Currently inconsistent: some surfaces gate (case Overview actions and the new add-entry FAB use 'active carer || supervisor'; team/admin screens use roles.dart helpers; aviary FAB uses canManage), while others don't (the per-tile edit/delete/overflow menus on every timeline tile — journal, weight, exam, condition, prescription, dose, marking, placement, follow-up, disposition — appear regardless of write permission).

Work:
- Establish ONE source of truth for 'can I write to this case' (e.g. a canEditCase(case, user) helper in core/auth/roles.dart, or a provider) and stop duplicating the inline 'activeCarer == me.id || supervisor' check (already copied in case_detail_screen _CaseActions and the add-entry FAB).
- Audit every write affordance and gate it: timeline tile edit/delete menus, the add-entry FAB (already done in xc8.1 — fold into the shared helper), case edit/status/share, sharing changes, aviary add/edit, animal edit, admin code-lists/team, profile, etc.
- Decide hide vs. disable per control (prefer hide for menus/FABs; disable+tooltip where the control must stay visible).
- Cover coordinator vs supervisor vs carer vs read-only-shared distinctions consistently (see existing roles.dart helpers).

Note: the org-scoped, private-by-default + opt-in sharing model means a user may have read-only access to a shared case — those viewers must see no write controls.
acceptance_criteria: A read-only viewer sees no write affordances (no add-entry FAB, no per-tile edit/delete menus, no edit/status/share) on cases shared read-only; permitted users see them as before; the case write check lives in one shared helper used everywhere; no UI offers an action the server will reject. Verified against the rule tests / a running stack where rules apply.
close_reason: Closed

title: Uploaded image doesn't appear until a manual refresh
description:
After adding/uploading a photo (e.g. a journal entry attachment), the image does not show immediately — it only appears after a pull-to-refresh, navigating away and back, or the next realtime tick. Looks like a race/refresh-ordering bug in the upload→display path.

Likely suspects to investigate:
- Provider refresh ordering: the create/update sheet invalidates the owning list provider (e.g. journalForCaseProvider) on save, but the new record's file field / the read-through cache may not yet reflect the just-uploaded file when the tile rebuilds.
- File token timing: protected file fields need a token from fileTokenProvider (FED-8.1); the attachment strip shows a spinner until repo+token resolve. If the token isn't ready, or the URL is built before it is, the first render can miss.
- Image cache: CachedFileImage keys by token-stripped URL (fileCacheKey, federfall-xu3). A freshly-created file id that was probed/cached as a 404/miss before the bytes existed could serve a stale negative result until evicted.
- Multipart create returns the record; confirm the attachments filename list on the returned RecordModel is used to build the URL immediately, rather than waiting for a re-fetch.

Repro: open a case → add a journal entry with a photo → save. Expected: the thumbnail appears in the new timeline tile right away. Actual: blank/absent until a manual refresh.

Touches: journal attachment strip (journal_entry_tile.dart _AttachmentStrip), the create/save + invalidate path (journal_entry_sheet.dart), fileTokenProvider, and CachedFileImage/fileCacheKey. Likely applies to other image fields too (exam/finder/clinical).
acceptance_criteria: A newly uploaded image renders in its tile immediately after save, without a manual refresh; no stale-cache miss; holds for journal attachments and any other image field that shares the path.
close_reason: Closed

title: Scope carer (Pflegestelle) visibility: only their own animals + aviaries
description:
Today the access rules (backend/pocketbase/pb_migrations/1700000010_access_rules.js) make animals, markings and aviaries org-wide readable by EVERY active member (list/view = 'org = @request.auth.org'). A 'carer' (role enum: carer|coordinator|supervisor — carer == Pflegestelle) should instead only see THEIR OWN data. Coordinators/supervisors keep the org-wide view.

REQUIREMENT
1. Aviaries: a carer sees only aviaries assigned to them. The 'aviaries' collection already has a 'keeper' relation -> users, so the carer branch is roughly: keeper = @request.auth.id (||
COORD_SUP for the wider roles). list+view rules.
2. Animals: a carer sees only 'their own' animals =
   (a) animals currently assigned to one of their aviaries — animals.current_aviary.keeper = @request.auth.id, AND
   (b) animals they have/had a case with — animals linked to a case where they are active_carer (cases_via_animal.active_carer ?= @request.auth.id). Confirm the exact back-relation name.

OPEN QUESTIONS (the '?' in the request)
- 'have (had?) a case with': active_carer is a single CURRENT field; past carers are not tracked except possibly via case_shares. Decide whether past involvement counts and, if so, how to express it (case_shares.shared_with? a carer-history?). 
- Re-identification tension: the rule comment + memory 'animals-created-via-intake-or-aviary-resident' treat animals/markings as an ORG-WIDE identity layer for re-identification/dedup. Scoping animal visibility per-carer may break carers re-identifying a returning bird. Decide: keep re-id server-side / coordinator-only, or leave markings org-wide while scoping animals?
- markings: scope alongside animals or keep org-wide for ring-number re-id?
- Does scoping animals break any current UI (animals registry, aviary detail resident list) for carers? Audit lib/features for queries that assume org-wide animals.

IMPLEMENTATION
- New migration that rewrites the animals + aviaries (and maybe markings) list/view rules with a role-gated branch: COORD_SUP -> org-wide; carer -> the scoped filter above. No schema change needed for aviaries (keeper exists) or animal-in-aviary (current_aviary exists); only a migration if 'had a case' needs new history.
- Rules are the real security boundary — add/extend cases in backend/pocketbase/tests/test_rules.py (run via tests/run.sh against a live PB) covering: carer sees own aviary + its residents + own-case animals; carer does NOT see another carer's aviary/animals; coordinator/supervisor still see all.

Relates to FED-1.11 (federfall-bhx, access rules) and Phase 8 hardening (federfall-49l).
close_reason: Decided the current org-wide animals/aviaries visibility model is fine as-is; carer-scoping not pursued.

title: Offline note shown spuriously after returning to the app
description:
After bringing the app back to the foreground, the offline note (core/connectivity/offline_banner.dart) sometimes appears even though the device is online — annoying false positive.

Likely cause in core/connectivity/connectivity.dart (onlineStatusProvider): on resume the server /health probe can transiently fail (network interface still waking up, or the probe times out) and latch OnlineStatus.offline, and re-evaluation only happens on a connectivity_plus interface change or the 30s heartbeat — so the stale 'offline' lingers. Returning from background may not emit an interface change, so nothing re-probes promptly.

Fix directions to evaluate:
- Re-probe on app lifecycle resume (AppLifecycleState.resumed), not just on interface change / heartbeat.
- Treat a single failed probe as tentative: require N consecutive failures (or a short retry) before showing offline, and re-check quickly after a failure rather than waiting for the next 30s tick.
- Avoid fl​ipping to offline during the brief resume window.

Acceptance: returning to the foreground while genuinely online does not flash the offline note; a real outage still surfaces it promptly. Touches connectivity.dart (onlineStatus) and possibly a lifecycle observer; offline_banner.dart unchanged.
close_reason: Closed

title: Timeline entries too condensed to read — give them room, especially exams
description:

Clinically important detail — especially exams (vitals + findings) — is hard to actually read at a glance.

Make entries more legible; it is fine for them to be bigger:
- Allow multi-line content (titles/notes not truncated to one line).
- Exams in particular should show their structured data readably (vitals, BCS, findings) rather than a one-line summary — consider an expanded inline layout or a richer card for exams.
- Keep the unified single-chronology pattern (one consistent TimelineItem look), just with more vertical room and clear hierarchy per kind.

Acceptance: a carer can read an exam's key values without opening it; long notes are not clipped; the timeline still reads as one consistent chronology. Touches timeline_item.dart, case_timeline.dart (the _Event tiles), and exams/exam_tile.dart.

title: Timeline 'add entry' needs a better UX than a tiny + → giant menu
description:
Adding a timeline entry (note, weight, condition, exam, prescription, dose, marking, handoff, placement, follow-up) is core, frequent functionality, but today it is a single small PopupMenuButton (Icons.add) in the History tab header that opens a long ~11-item popup menu (case_timeline.dart:162). That is poor discoverability and a lot of scanning for the most-used actions.

Design a better add-entry UX. Options to evaluate:
- A prominent FAB (or extended FAB) on the History tab instead of a header icon.

design:

The most context-bound kind (Dosis) also stays inline on its prescription tile. One consistent surface — no fragmentation.

Implemented across the child issues (phases 1-4 + inline dose). Phase 1 alone satisfies this epic's acceptance.

title: Evaluate Protected file fields + tokens for case/animal photos
description:
PocketBase serves file fields PUBLICLY by full URL by default (the random
filename suffix is the only guard). Sensitive files can be marked **Protected**
in the field options, then fetched with a short-lived **file token**
(pb.files.getToken() → append ?token=... to the URL).

Today every image (intake_photos on cases, journal_entries.attachments,
animals.photo) uses an UNPROTECTED field — PbRepository.fileUrl() builds a
plain /api/files URL with no token (the doc-comment even notes "the file field
is unprotected, so the URL is usable without an auth token"). So anyone with
the URL can view a patient/finder photo, bypassing the org-scoped access rules
that protect the records themselves.

TASK:
1. Decide which file fields should be Protected. Clinical/finder-linked photos
   (case intake_photos, journal attachments, possibly animal photo) likely
   should be, to match the private-by-default record model. Pure-public assets
   (none currently) stay open.
2. Backend: migration to set `protected: true` on those file fields.
3. Client: fileUrl() must request and append a file token for protected fields
   (pb.files.getToken(), cache it ~the token TTL, append ?token=). Image.network
   needs the tokenised URL; the full-screen viewer + share path too. Handle
   token refresh/expiry and offline (cached images already fetched are fine;
   new fetches need a token → require connectivity).
4. Verify with backend rule tests: a protected file URL without a valid token
   is rejected; with a token from an authorized user it works; another org's
   user cannot get a usable token.

Touches: pb_migrations (protected flag), packages/federfall_data PbRepository
.fileUrl + a token helper, image_viewer + thumbnails + animal_avatar, tests.
Note: protected files are NOT thumbnail-cacheable by plain URL, and the token
is short-lived — factor that into the thumbnail/loading code.
close_reason: Protected the three clinical/finder-linked file fields (cases.intake_photos, journal_entries.attachments, animals.photo) via migration 1700000027; client appends a short-lived file token (fileTokenProvider -> pb.files.getToken(), ~90s cache) to file URLs via fileUrl(token:). Verified by backend rule tests (bare URL rejected, same-org token works, cross-org token rejected) + data-layer unit test. Thumbnail cache-churn deferred to federfall-xu3.
