title: Exam: add weight + temperature; route intake weight to a Weight entry; drop dead case clinical fields
design:
GOAL
Reconcile the case's clinical fields with the relational exam/weights model.
Weight + temperature + mucous membranes become exam/weights data; the dead
exam_* columns and the case-only intake_weight_g are removed. Validated against
docs/REQUIREMENTS.md (lines 162, 168) — temperature/mm are spec'd; intake weight
is a spec headline fast-path field (lines 79, 281, 341), so it STAYS on the
intake form but writes a Weight entry instead of a case column.

PRINCIPLE: every exam datum is its own typed, optional field, persisted ONLY
when the user enters it (sparse). NOTHING is folded into free-text notes.

PART A — weight on the exam
- Optional weight field in the exam sheet vitals zone. On save, if entered,
  CREATE a Weight (animal, case, weight_g, measured_at = examined_at) via
  weightsRepository. Not stored on the exam record — `weights` stays the single
  source of truth + trend. Invalidate weightsForCaseProvider.
- Create-only on the exam-CREATE path (hide/disable on edit) to avoid dup; a
  weights.exam relation for true edit-sync is a later option.

PART B — temperature + mucous membranes (new clinical vitals, all spec-listed)
- Add to the exams collection + Exam model + sheet vitals zone, each optional
  and stored only when entered:
  - `temperature` (number, nullable; °C). Birds normal ~40-42 °C.
  - `mm_color` (select: pink/pale/cyanotic/icteric/injected) — new wire enum.
  - `mm_texture` (select: moist/tacky/dry) — new wire enum.
  (Free-text optional fields are the lighter alt to the selects, if preferred.)

PART C — intake weight: KEEP the field, change what it writes
- new_case_screen keeps ONE weight input (fast-path per spec). In _create,
  after the case is created, if a weight was entered, create a Weight
  (animal, case = created.id, weight_g, measured_at = admitted_at) instead of
  writing case.intake_weight_g.
- Note: if the optional entry exam (blp.4) is then opened, leave its weight
  field blank to avoid a duplicate at the same timestamp (or prefill from the
  just-created Weight). Minor; accept or refine.
- Remove the weight field from edit_case_intake_sheet (weights are edited on
  the timeline now).

PART D — remove dead case clinical columns
- The 14 cases.exam_* columns (exam_bcs, exam_dehydration, exam_attitude,
  exam_temperature, exam_mm_color, exam_mm_texture, exam_head, exam_cns,
  exam_cardiopulmonary, exam_gi, exam_musculoskeletal, exam_integument,
  exam_forelimb, exam_hindlimb) are mapped in the Case model but never read or
  written by any UI — pure vestige of the original flat-exam FED-4.8 plan, now
  superseded by exams/exam_findings. Drop them (migration) + remove the fields
  from medical_case.dart. They are empty (no writer) → NO backfill needed.
- intake_weight_g: keep the column for old rows; add a one-off migration
  backfilling existing intake_weight_g -> Weight (animal, case,
  measured_at = admitted_at) so historic intake weights reach the trend, then
  stop writing it (PART C). Drop the column + Case.intakeWeightG in a later
  migration once nothing reads it.
- case_detail_screen.dart:361 currently shows intake_weight_g — switch to read
  the earliest/latest Weight from the trend, or drop the standalone display.

TOUCHES: exams migration (temperature/mm_color/mm_texture), exam_sheet, exam
model + enums (MmColor, MmTexture), new_case_screen (+test), edit_case_intake_
sheet (+test), case_detail_screen (+test), medical_case.dart (+mapper test),
weights provider/repo, l10n + cases_labels; drop-exam_* migration +
intake_weight_g backfill migration.
close_reason: Exam now captures weight (→Weight entry, create-path), temperature + mucous-membrane vitals (typed selects). Intake weight kept as fast-path but writes a Weight entry; removed from edit sheet + case detail. Dropped 14 dead cases.exam_* columns + Case model fields; backfill migration intake_weight_g→weights. migrate up clean, 73/73 rule tests, models 17/17, app 224/224.

title: First-run: require a VERIFIED federfall server before login, and let it inform login options
description:
On mobile/native first start the user must configure the backend before the login screen appears (the gate already does native→/setup→/login). Two gaps remain:

1. GENUINE-FEDERFALL VERIFICATION. ServerProbe today only checks GET /api/health == 200, which ANY PocketBase (or anything returning a 200 health) passes — ProbeNotFederfall never fires for a generic PB. Add a federfall identity/capabilities endpoint (pb_hooks route, e.g. GET /api/federfall/info, unauthenticated) returning a federfall marker + schema/app version + min supported client + login options. Upgrade ServerProbe to require that marker before ProbeReachable; reject generic PB / random 200s with a clear 'not a Federfall server' message (server_probe.dart already has ProbeNotFederfall).

2. SERVER INFORMS LOGIN. The verified server's capabilities should drive the login screen: which auth methods are enabled (password, OAuth providers), self-signup vs invite-only, password-reset availability, org/branding name. Expose the fetched capabilities via a provider; the router gate must ensure they resolve before /login renders (login adapts its buttons/links accordingly). Optionally surface 'update required' when min-client > current build.

Touches: backend pb_hooks (new info route), core/server/server_probe.dart, server_config_controller.dart, features/server_setup/setup_screen.dart, features/auth/login_screen.dart, router gate. Acceptance: first start forces server entry; a non-federfall URL is rejected; once a verified server is set the login screen reflects that server's enabled options; config persists across restarts; rule/probe tests cover federfall-vs-generic-PB.
close_reason: Closed

title: medication_due view + worklist single-query fetch (perf)
description: Worklist N+1: meds+doses fetched per case. Add a medication_due PB view computing next_due per active prescription (join latest administration; scheduled=last_dose+interval_hours, once=started_at if never given), org+active_carer scoped. Client: drop per-case meds/doses fetch (one view query), collapse follow-ups + animals to single filtered list() queries. buildWorklist consumes MedicationDue (next_due precomputed); next-due correctness moves to the backend rule test.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add animals without a case (aviary-only residents)
description: Today an animal identity is created only via case intake. Allow adding an animal directly — e.g. a permanent/aviary resident that never had an acute case — so it can be an aviary member without a case. Needs: a create-animal entry point (animals registry FAB and/or aviary detail 'add resident'), the animal create flow (species/sex/name/markings), and optionally placing it straight into an aviary (lifetime_status=in_aviary, current_aviary). Verify access rules allow case-less animals.
close_reason: Closed

title: Record time-of-day on doses & journal entries (worklist prereq)
description: Medication administrations and journal entries store a full DateTime but the UI only picks a date, zeroing the time. 'Next-due' med scheduling (cr3.1 worklist) needs the actual time a dose was given. Add a date+time picker (shared pickDateTime + DateField showTime) and display time on the administration & journal timeline tiles. Convert UTC->local for display/picking.
close_reason: Closed

title: Handle quarantine times (not yet implemented)
description: Quarantine is only partially handled. The FED-1.12 hook stamps a default quarantine_until (admitted_at + 14 days) on case create, and the dashboard surfaces 'quarantine ending soon', but there is no way to: set/edit the quarantine period per case (species/reason-dependent), see quarantine state clearly on the case, mark quarantine complete/lifted, or factor it into the worklist (UX Phase D). Decide the quarantine model (fixed default vs editable; what 'ending' triggers) and build the UI + any hook/schema changes. Surfaced during the UX-90 push.
close_reason: Quarantine shown on case Overview (with ended state) + editable/liftable via intake sheet (future-date picker). Worklist integration deferred to Phase D.

title: Rethink app navigation / information architecture

The 4-tab shell (Dashboard/Cases/Animals/Aviaries) plus app-bar actions (profile, admin, stats) plus growing admin sub-routes (members, org settings, conditions) and per-screen FABs/menus lack a coherent IA. Audit the whole navigation surface and propose a cleaner structure: where admin/org functions live (one 'Admin' hub vs scattered app-bar icons), how settings/profile are reached, whether reporting belongs in the shell, and how deep actions (new case, handoff, worklist) surface. Deliverable: an IA proposal + the refactor. Cross-cutting across all UX phases.
close_reason: Closed

title: Edit animal identity (name/species/sex)

title: Header avatar: animal picture w/ fallback to latest case admission photo
description: Add a round avatar to the shared detail header. Source order: (1) an explicit animal picture if set, else (2) the most recent case's first intake photo for that animal, else (3) a placeholder (pets icon). Allow setting/replacing a dedicated animal picture that is SEPARATE from case intake photos. Needs backend: a new 'photo' file field on the animals collection (new migration; animals are org-wide readable so the file inherits that). Model: add Animal.photo + fileUrl access via animals repo. UI: round avatar in the header (tap to view/replace on the animal detail; read-only on the case header). Reuse the existing image_picker injection (imagePickerProvider) and createWithFiles/updateWithFiles + fileUrl(thumb) patterns. Fallback-to-latest-case-photo should reuse case_summaries/cases for the animal.

Editable (gallery/camera/remove) from both animal & case headers. Backend animals.photo file field (migration 1700000017) verified on live PB (upload+readback rule test, 50 checks). title: Shared case/animal detail header (adopt case-overview style)

Extract ONE shared header widget and use it in both. Adopt the case-overview style as the canonical look (name-first headline, species/subtitle line, status chip). The case header shows case status (CaseStatus); the animal header shows lifetime status (LifetimeStatus) — parameterise the chip/subtitle so one widget serves both. Prefer a widget in lib/ui or features/cases shared spot.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A single header widget renders both case detail and animal detail. 2) Visual style matches today's case-overview header. 3) Status chip is correct per screen (case vs lifetime status). 4) No regression in existing case/animal detail widget tests; analyze clean.
close_reason: Extracted DetailHeader (lib/ui/widgets/detail_header.dart): title + optional subtitle + optional status chip + optional leading slot (for ctw.7). Case _Header and animal _Identity both delegate to it; animal screen dropped its Card and centres the header block but keeps identical case-overview internals. 3 widget tests; analyze clean, 145 tests green.

title: Decide case status lifecycle — are in_treatment/rehab/ready_for_release needed?
description: The cases.status enum (migration 1700000004_cases.js) defines in_care | in_treatment | rehab | ready_for_release | disposed, but only two values are ever set, by hooks in pb_hooks/main.pb.js: in_care (default on case create) and disposed (on a terminal disposition). The three middle stages are unreachable — no app UI and no hook transitions a case into them. The app only reads status (list, detail chip, dashboard). REQUIREMENTS.md:164 calls status 'derived/maintained'; the dashboard (REQUIREMENTS.md:289 / FED-7.1) breaks down 'cases by status', so today it would always show in_care=N and the other active stages 0.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Decide whether the intermediate stages (in_treatment/rehab/ready_for_release) are needed at all, or whether in_care + disposed suffices. 2) If KEPT: add a transition mechanism (manual status picker on case detail and/or derive from clinical state, e.g. ready_for_release when is_releasable, in_treatment when active meds). 3) If DROPPED: new migration removing them from the enum, plus remove labels (cases_labels.dart + l10n) and the dashboard breakdown rows. 4) Revisit the FED-7.1 dashboard breakdown either way (note: empty rows were hidden as an interim fix).
close_reason: Decided: 3-state lifecycle in_care -> ready_for_release -> disposed. Drop in_treatment/rehab (treatment detail lives in the timeline). ready_for_release is a manual flag; disposed set by disposition hook.

title: FED-7.6 — Animal lifetime detail (all cases)
description: One animal's full lifetime record: identity (name/species/sex), markings (active + historic), lifetime status, and ALL its cases (current + historic) newest-first. Reuse casesForAnimal + markingsForAnimal. Inaccessible cases (held by another carer, not shared) render as a non-tappable stub showing case number + status + dates only (no clinical detail); accessible cases open into case detail. Reachable from the Animals registry and from a case (animal link).
close_reason: Animal lifetime detail: identity (name/species/sex + lifetime-status chip), all markings (active+historic), every case newest-first. Accessible cases tap into case detail; inaccessible ones render as non-tappable stubs (number/status/dates + no-access badge) sourced from a new org-wide case_summaries PB view (migration 1700000016) that projects only non-clinical columns. New CaseSummary model + repo + animalLifetime provider. Verified on live PB: 5 new rule tests (48 total) prove summary readable org-wide, no clinical leak, org isolation. Flutter mapper+widget tests green.

title: FED-7.5 — Animals registry (list + search)
description: Searchable list of the org's animals (persistent identities, org-wide readable). Row shows name, species, active marking code(s), lifetime status (in_care / at_large_released / in_aviary / deceased). Search by name or ring/chip code (reuse markings.activeByCode + animals.searchByName). Carers still see their animals here. Tap -> animal lifetime detail. Keep aviary residency in mind for later (Phase 6) but do not build aviary features now.
close_reason: Animals registry: searchable list of org animals (org-wide readable). Row shows name, species, active marking codes, lifetime status. Search matches name or active ring/chip code. Joins animals+active markings in one fetch; pure filterAnimals() unit-tested + widget tests. Rows tap to animal lifetime detail via new /animals/:id route (placeholder screen now, body filled by FED-7.6). Added lifetimeStatusLabel + l10n. Unblocks FED-7.6.

title: FED-7.4 — All-cases browser (filters + search)
description: Browse all cases the user can access (server rules scope it: carer = own + shared; coordinator/supervisor = all in org). DEFAULT VIEW = the user's own/active cases ('My cases'); an 'All' toggle/filter widens to everything they can access. Filters: status (incl. disposed/historic), active-vs-closed, species, date range; plus text search (case number / animal name). Tap-through to case detail. Access-scoped, not a new permission.
close_reason: All-cases browser: scope toggle (mine/all, server-scoped), activity (active/closed/all), species, admission date-range filters + text search (case no / animal name). Compact UI — search always visible, secondary filters in a badged filter bottom-sheet that updates live. Pure filterCases() over one client-side fetch (cases+animals); unit + widget tests. Replaced dead myCasesProvider; writes now refresh casesBrowserData+dashboardSummary. Dashboard->browser deep-link pre-filter filed as follow-up.

title: FED-7.0 — App navigation shell (adaptive)
description: Replace the single 'My cases' home with an adaptive top-level navigation: Material NavigationBar (bottom) on narrow screens and NavigationRail on wide/web. Destinations: Dashboard, Cases, Animals. Profile + supervisor Admin stay reachable from the app bar. Prerequisite for the rest of Phase 7.
close_reason: Adaptive nav shell: StatefulShellRoute (Dashboard/Cases/Animals) with NavigationBar<600w / NavigationRail>=600w (extended>=840). Profile+admin via shared AccountActions in each tab app bar. HomeScreen split into CasesScreen + Dashboard/Animals placeholders. Tests + analyze green.

title: Semantic medication frequency (reminder-ready)
description: Replace free-text medication frequency with a structured interval model so reminders can be built later (requirements: keep dates structured). Add frequency_kind (once|scheduled|as_needed) + interval_hours to medications; prescription form uses interval presets (q24/q12/q8/q6/q48/custom) + once/PRN; tile shows a derived label. Follow-up to FED-4.6.
close_reason: Medication frequency is now structured + reminder-ready: medications gains frequency_kind (once|scheduled|as_needed) + interval_hours (migration). Prescription form uses interval presets (once/q24/q12/q8/q6/q48/custom/PRN) mapping to (kind, hours); tile shows a derived label (e.g. 'Twice daily', 'Every 10 h'). Free-text frequency kept as an optional note. A reminder = last administration + interval_hours. Tests for default+chosen preset and tile label.

title: Tidy case detail into Overview/History tabs
description: Case detail was growing too long (header + intake + chart + timeline stacked). Split into a persistent name-first header over two tabs: Overview (intake summary + weight trend) and History (the unified chronology). User-chosen layout.
close_reason: Case detail is now a persistent name-first header over two tabs: Overview (weight trend + intake summary) and History (the unified CaseTimeline, title suppressed since the tab names it). Keeps the screen short as more record kinds land. Detail test updated to switch tabs.

Intake milestones and journal entries interleave newest-first; future records (weights/meds/conditions/dispositions) plug in as additional event kinds. Refinement of FED-4.3/FED-4.7.

Events interleave newest-first; future record kinds slot in. Replaces the separate JournalSection/_TimelineSection. Tests for ordering + empty state.

title: Journal entry edit & delete

Added updateWithFiles() (multipart, keep-list pattern) to the repo; generalized the entry sheet to create-or-edit; per-entry overflow menu for edit/delete. Tests for edit (drops removed attachment), delete, and updateWithFiles.

Remaining: wire intake_photos into the FED-4.1 intake form using the same infra (pick photos at intake, upload as multipart on case create). Small UI task now.

title: Security: geocode routes missing the guest-role check other routes have
description: geocode.pb.js forward/reverse routes require only $apis.requireAuth(), so authenticated guests — walled off from all data everywhere else, and auto-created via OAuth2 self-registration — can drive the server-side geocoder and burn the upstream Nominatim budget (OWASP A01, resource abuse). intake.pb.js and exam.pb.js explicitly reject role === 'guest'; geocode should match. Fix: add the same guest rejection to both geocode handlers (defined INSIDE each handler — JSVM isolated-context gotcha). Found in 2026-07-03 OWASP review.
acceptance_criteria: Guest token gets 403 from /api/federfall/geocode/* ; regular member unaffected; covered by a test.
close_reason: Fixed: both geocode routes (geocode.pb.js) now reject guest-role tokens with 403, matching intake.pb.js/exam.pb.js. Check runs before any upstream Nominatim call. Covered by 2 new test_rules.py assertions. 220/220 backend tests pass.

title: Security: harden OAuth2 bootstrap-supervisor path (zero-active-supervisor grants next sign-in supervisor)
description: oauth2_provisioning.pb.js: 'first user becomes supervisor' triggers whenever NO ACTIVE supervisor currently exists, and that path also bypasses FEDERFALL_OIDC_ALLOWED_GROUPS gating. The lockout guard in main.pb.js makes reaching zero active supervisors hard, but Admin-UI/superuser batch ops can get there — after which the NEXT OAuth2 sign-in (anyone at the IdP) is auto-provisioned supervisor. Two concurrent first sign-ins can also both win the race (OWASP A07). Fix: key bootstrap on a stricter 'instance never initialised' signal (e.g. no users at all, or a one-shot flag), keep ALLOWED_GROUPS gating applied even for the bootstrap user, and make the promotion race-safe. Related: federfall-8qz (oauth2 provisioning integration tests). Found in 2026-07-03 OWASP review.
acceptance_criteria: With existing users but zero active supervisors, an OAuth2 sign-in outside allowed groups lands as guest, not supervisor; true first-boot flow still works; concurrent first sign-ins yield exactly one supervisor.
close_reason: Fixed in oauth2_provisioning.pb.js: (1) 'first user' now keyed on 'no users record exists at all' instead of 'no active supervisor right now', so a batch-deactivated instance can't be re-bootstrapped by the next arbitrary OAuth2 sign-in — recovery stays on the operator-only bootstrap_supervisor.pb.js env path. (2) FEDERFALL_OIDC_ALLOWED_GROUPS gating now applies to the bootstrap user too. (3) Concurrent first sign-ins are reconciled post-commit (earliest created+id wins, later one demoted to guest) since the JSVM has no cross-request mutex. No automated test: OAuth2 flow test infra doesn't exist yet (tracked separately in federfall-8qz) — verified by code review + confirming the hook still loads/starts cleanly (run.sh boots successfully, 220/220 other tests pass).

title: Security: validate to_user on placement handoff (exists, active, same-org)
description: main.pb.js placements onRecordCreate sets cases.active_carer to the raw to_user id without checking the target exists, is_active, and belongs to the case's org (intake.pb.js and exam.pb.js DO validate referenced records). A handoff to a bad/foreign/deactivated id orphans the case — invisible to everyone but coordinators/supervisors and assigned to a user who can't act (OWASP A01, integrity). Not a cross-org data leak (case view rule still gates on org). Fix: inside the placements hook, load to_user and require user.org === caseRec.org && user.is_active before reassigning; reject otherwise. Add a rule test. Found in 2026-07-03 OWASP review.
acceptance_criteria: Placement with to_user that is nonexistent, deactivated, or another org's user is rejected server-side; valid handoffs unaffected; covered in test_rules.py.
close_reason: Fixed: placements hook (main.pb.js) now validates to_user exists, is_active, and matches the case's org before deriving active_carer; rejects otherwise. Covered by 4 new assertions in test_rules.py (nonexistent/foreign-org/deactivated to_user + active_carer unchanged). 220/220 backend tests pass.

title: Idempotency key for POST /api/federfall/intake
description: Follow-up to federfall-kazj: the client now reports timed-out writes as unknownOutcome so users are warned before retrying, but a true fix is server-side idempotency — client sends a UUID key with the intake payload, the hook stores it (e.g. unique column or short-lived table) and returns the original result on replay instead of creating a second animal+case. Files: backend/pocketbase/pb_hooks/intake.pb.js, packages/federfall_data/lib/src/repositories/cases_repository.dart. Same pattern would cover /api/federfall/exam creates.
close_reason: idempotency_key on POST /api/federfall/intake: stored with response in the intake transaction (idempotency_keys collection, per-user unique, 24h TTL + purge cron); replay returns original result. Wizard sends one stable key per intake; keyed timeout now a plain network error. Exam route left for a follow-up if wanted. Commit a895685.

title: Dashboard intake KPI and its drill-down disagree on Dec 31
description:
File: apps/federfall/lib/features/dashboard/dashboard_screen.dart:194-201

The tile counts admitted.year == now.year but the tap-through filter range ends at DateTime(year, 12, 31) — midnight, excluding anything admitted during Dec 31. On New Year's Eve the KPI number and the filtered case list it opens differ, which reads as a data bug.

title: Org settings silently coerce empty numeric fields to defaults
description:
File: apps/federfall/lib/features/admin/org_settings_screen.dart:109-112

A supervisor who blanks a field believing it "unsets" it saves a default GDPR-retention window without noticing; "0 months" saves but is meaningless.

description:
File: packages/federfall_data/lib/src/pb_repository.dart:67 and :79

Every in-package query correctly binds params via filterExpr (pb.filter) — no injection found — but the base API lets any app-layer caller pass an interpolated string (e.g. a search box straight into 'name ~ "$q"'), and nothing in the types nudges them toward filterExpr.

Files: packages/federfall_data/lib/src/repositories/animal_species_repository.dart:30, case_summaries_repository.dart, case_activity_repository.dart, quarantine_repository.dart (PbCaseQuarantineRepository), clinical_repositories.dart (PbMedicationDueRepository)

All view-backed repos extend PbRepository<T>, so create/update/delete are statically available and only fail at runtime against the PB view. A ReadOnlyRepository<T> split of the Repository interface would make misuse a compile error.

File: apps/federfall/lib/core/error/error_message.dart:18

title: Admin/supervisor routes gated in UI only — add role redirect in router _gate
description: /manage, /manage/team, /org-settings, code-list admin screens and /statistics (apps/federfall/lib/routing/router.dart:241-272) have no role check in _gate (router.dart:289), which only distinguishes unauthenticated/guest/real-role. roles.dart (canManageTeam, canViewReports) gates entry points but not routes, so a plain carer navigating directly gets the screen rendered. Not a data breach — server access rules are the real boundary — but a defense-in-depth/UX gap: carer reaches a control surface that fails server-side. Fix: role checks in _gate keyed on currentUser.role, redirecting non-privileged users home.
close_reason: Fixed in 3d31bf4 (P2+P3 bundle): GlobalKey handoff across 840px, worklist source/derived split with clock-only tick, router role redirects, parallel browser fetches, aviary liveRefresh, rootLogger funnel, 404 l10n. flutter analyze clean, 322 tests green.

title: No Content-Security-Policy on web SPA
description: No CSP in apps/federfall/web/index.html and web_headers.pb.js only sets COOP/COEP. Given the app serves user-uploaded files and stores the bearer token in localStorage on web (federfall-xe9), CSP is the main missing mitigation — it caps the blast radius of the MIME-type upload gap (federfall-8a5) and any future DOM injection. Fix: in pb_hooks/web_headers.pb.js (already special-cases SPA vs /api and /_), add Content-Security-Policy for SPA paths: default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data: <MAP_TILE_URL host>; connect-src 'self'; script-src per Flutter wasm/canvaskit needs; object-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'none'. Must be tested against the wasm bootstrap. Related: federfall-xe9, federfall-8a5.
close_reason: Strict same-origin CSP on SPA paths + 'default-src none; sandbox' on /api/files/ in web_headers.pb.js. Bundle made fully same-origin (external flutter_bootstrap.js, --no-web-resources-cdn, bundled Roboto). Verified headless against the real wasm bundle: boots, crossOriginIsolated, zero violations, zero external requests. Emoji-fallback tofu tradeoff documented; FEDERFALL_CSP override available. Commit b3c941d.

title: Geocode rate limit counts the reverse proxy's IP unless trustedProxy is set
description: PocketBase's rate limiter (used for /api/federfall/geocode since federfall-0tf) keys on the client IP, but behind the documented Caddy/nginx reverse proxy PocketBase sees the proxy's address unless settings.trustedProxy is configured — so the 30/60s geocode budget is shared by ALL users instead of per client. Still protects the upstream geocoder, but per-user fairness is off. Fix: apply trustedProxy headers (e.g. X-Forwarded-For from the local proxy) via settings.pb.js env (FEDERFALL_TRUSTED_PROXY_HEADERS?) and document it in DEPLOYMENT.md next to the reverse-proxy section.
close_reason: FEDERFALL_TRUSTED_PROXY_HEADERS (+_USE_LEFTMOST_IP) applied via settings.pb.js on boot; documented in docker-compose.yml + DEPLOYMENT.md. Commit 34a624a.

title: UX: weight tile offers delete to members who can no longer delete
description: Since migration 1700000047, weights deleteRule is author-or-supervisor, but weight_entry_tile.dart still shows the delete menu action to any member — a non-author now gets a server error snackbar instead of not seeing the option. Hide (or disable) delete unless the current user is the weight's author or a supervisor.
close_reason: weight_entry_tile.dart gates the delete menu item on weightDeletableBy(weight, me) (new helper in core/auth/roles.dart): only the weight's author or a supervisor sees it, mirroring migration 1700000047. Edit stays gated by canEdit as before. Widget tests cover author-deletes, non-author-hidden, supervisor-sees-delete.

title: Security: case create can link an arbitrary existing finder (PII exposure by id)
description: Sibling of federfall-621, on the CREATE side where :isset guards can't help: the cases createRule only checks org + active_carer = auth, and the finders view rule grants PII access via cases_via_finder.active_carer ?= auth. Any member who learns a finder record id (logs, shared exports, a revoked share they once held) can create a throwaway case with finder=<victimId> and read the finder's PII. Mitigated by unguessable 15-char ids, but the rule should require the linked finder to be reachable by the creator (or finder linking should move to a hook that checks visibility). Also: cases create doesn't require finder.org to match, so a cross-org finder link is accepted.
close_reason: Closed by the federfall-zod fix: cases.finder can no longer be set or re-pointed by clients (migration 1700000044); linking happens only inside the intake route with server-controlled finder creation.

comments.author: Jan-Henrik Bruhn
comments.text: Decision (2026-07-02): fold into federfall-zod's atomic intake endpoint rather than fixing standalone. When intake goes through a server-side transaction route, regular clients never set cases.finder, so the field can be made fully non-writable via ':isset = false' guards on create AND update (extends migration 1700000043). This removes the attack surface instead of gating it (the hook-guard / created_by alternatives were considered and rejected as interim). Blocked on zod; close together.

title: UX: quarantine 'ends today' worklist cue disappears if the app isn't opened that day
description: buildWorklist (worklist.dart) only emits quarantineEnding when the end falls on the current calendar day — deliberately never 'overdue' so it can't nag forever. But a carer who doesn't open the app that day gets no release cue at all afterwards; the case just goes quiet until the 7-day stale marker. Consider a short grace window (e.g. show 'ended N days ago' for a few days, still neutral severity) so a missed end-day still surfaces once.
close_reason: buildWorklist emits quarantineEnding for ends up to quarantineGraceDays=3 in the past, still neutral/upcoming; label shows 'Ended yesterday / N days ago' (worklistQuarantineEndedDaysAgo). Tests updated.

title: UX: aviary over-capacity is not surfaced
description: aviary_detail_screen.dart shows the occupancy chip via aviaryOccupancyOfCapacity (e.g. '12 / 10') with no visual warning when residents exceed capacity, and the aviaries list tile shows capacity but not occupancy at all. Highlight the chip (error/tertiary color) when over capacity, and consider occupancy in the list tile so a coordinator can spot the full aviary before choosing it in the disposition sheet's aviary picker.
close_reason: Detail header chip renders in errorContainer colors when residents > capacity (DetailHeader.chipAlert); aviaries list shows occupancy '3 / 8' via new aviaryOccupancyCounts provider plus an error icon when over. Disposition-sheet aviary picker left as-is (list now surfaces the full aviary beforehand).

title: UX: password set (invite/reset) lacks client-side min-length validation
description: confirm_reset_screen.dart only validates required + match; a too-short password round-trips to PocketBase and comes back as a generic validation error. Add a min-length validator (PocketBase default is 8) with a specific message; also applies to any future change-password flow.
close_reason: Closed

title: UX: sign-out is a single unconfirmed tap
description: profile_screen.dart: the sign-out PrimaryButton fires immediately; one accidental tap logs the user out (and once federfall-4o4 lands, purges the photo cache). Add a lightweight confirm dialog. Same applies to the sign-out TextButton on pending_approval_screen.dart (lower stakes there).
close_reason: Shared confirmSignOut() dialog in core/auth/sign_out.dart; wired into profile_screen and pending_approval_screen. New l10n key authSignOutConfirm.

title: UX: CSV export error shows raw exception text
description: statistics_screen.dart _exportCsv catch block does messenger.showSnackBar(SnackBar(content: Text('$e'))) — the user sees a raw Dart exception string. Use errorMessage(l10n, e) like everywhere else.
close_reason: CSV export catch now shows errorMessage(l10n, e) instead of the raw exception (statistics_screen.dart).

title: UX: cases search doesn't match ring/chip codes
description: filterCases (cases_browser.dart) matches free text only against case number and animal name, while the Animals tab search also matches active ring/chip codes. A carer holding a ringed bird will naturally search the ring code on the Cases tab and get nothing — they must know to switch to Animals. Include the animal's active marking codes in the cases text match (data is already loaded per animal for the animals registry; needs markings joined into CasesBrowserData or a shared lookup).
close_reason: Closed

title: UX: case list tile shows 'Neuer Fall' as title for unnumbered cases
description: cases_screen.dart _CaseTile: title is 'medicalCase.caseNumber ?? l10n.caseNewTitle', so a case without a number renders as 'Neuer Fall' in the list — reads like a create button, not a case. Add a dedicated 'no number yet' string (or fall back to the animal label).
close_reason: Unnumbered case tile is titled by the animal label (fallback l10n.worklistUnnumberedCase); animal drops out of the subtitle to avoid duplication.

title: UX: missing date cross-validation — found_at after admitted_at, quarantine end before start
description: No cross-field date checks: the intake wizard and edit_case_intake_sheet.dart allow found_at > admitted_at, and quarantine_sheet.dart has no 'until > set_at' validation, so a quarantine can end before it began (producing a nonsense started/ended pair on the timeline). Add inline validation on save.
close_reason: Closed

title: Logic: a supervisor can demote/deactivate the last remaining supervisor (lockout)
description:
The users update rules and main.pb.js field guard allow a supervisor to change role/is_active on any user including the last supervisor; nothing counts remaining active supervisors. Recovery exists via the env bootstrap (bootstrap_supervisor.pb.js), but a guard would prevent the lockout instead of curing it.

Fix: in the users onRecordUpdateRequest hook, block demoting/deactivating the final active supervisor.
close_reason: Lockout guard in main.pb.js: users onRecordUpdateRequest + onRecordDeleteRequest block demoting/deactivating/moving/deleting the last active supervisor of an org (applies to superusers too; env bootstrap stays the recovery path). Tested in test_rules.py [last-supervisor lockout guard].

title: Docs drift: CLAUDE.md 'read-through offline cache' + info.pb.js version pinning
description:
1) CLAUDE.md says PbRepository has a 'read-through offline cache'; the code is explicitly online-only (pb_repository.dart:31). 2) info.pb.js hand-maintains VERSION/MIN_CLIENT at '1.0.0' with nothing tying them to the app pubspec, so the update-required mechanism will silently never fire.

Fix: correct the CLAUDE.md description; wire minClient/version to a real source or document the manual-bump process.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Jan-Henrik Bruhn
comments.text: Part 1 (CLAUDE.md 'read-through offline cache' wording) fixed: architecture section now states online-only, no local cache, networkTimeout fail-fast; also removed stale 'shared cache' from the repo-providers line. Part 2 (info.pb.js VERSION/MIN_CLIENT pinned to nothing) still open — keep this issue for that.

comments.author: Jan-Henrik Bruhn
comments.text: Part 2 fixed: info.pb.js's VERSION now reads $os.getenv("FEDERFALL_VERSION") (baked into the image at build time from the release-please tag via the new .github/workflows/release-please.yml), instead of a hand-maintained literal. Public /api/federfall/info now reports major.minor only (patch withheld for fingerprinting reasons). MIN_CLIENT stays a deliberately manual policy value, documented as such in both the hook and CLAUDE.md's new Releases section. Closing.

title: UX: share sheet cannot change access level; revoke has no confirm or double-tap guard
description:
case_share_sheet.dart — an existing share's access level can't be edited; user must revoke and re-grant. Revoke (_revoke) fires immediately with no confirmation and no busy-guard against double taps.

Fix: allow editing access on an existing share; add a confirm + in-flight guard to revoke.
close_reason: Closed

title: Security: web build stores bearer token in localStorage (XSS exposure) — document tradeoff
description: PrefsAuthTokenStorage (apps/federfall/lib/core/pocketbase/auth_token_storage.dart:46) keeps the auth payload in localStorage on web, exposed to any XSS. The code comment acknowledges this (no real secure storage on web, matches PB JS SDK). Filing so it stays a conscious, documented decision; consider a short-TTL token + refresh, or in-memory + httpOnly cookie if a proxy is added.
close_reason: Fixed in commits f4cdec6, 56a39f4, 813f45b, 0b34ceb, 8341830

comments.author: Jan-Henrik Bruhn
comments.text: OWASP review 2026-07-03 confirmed the tradeoff is defensible (canvas rendering, no inline scripts, backend script-src 'self' CSP) — but it makes pb_hooks/web_headers.pb.js security-critical: the localStorage decision leans on that CSP staying deployed. Document that invariant here; note FEDERFALL_CSP='off' disables it operator-side.

title: Logic: weights org-wide writable AND deletable by any member (inconsistent with animals/markings)
description:
1700000020_weights_animal_scoped.js:54-59 makes weights create/update/delete org-wide for any member, while the other identity-layer collections (animals, markings) reserve delete for supervisors (1700000010_access_rules.js:70). Any carer can silently delete another carer's clinical weight history.

Fix: restrict weights deleteRule to supervisor (or the record author), matching markings/animals.
close_reason: Migration 1700000047: weights deleteRule = supervisor OR author (guest-safe predicate). Author is populated by both the app sheet and the intake hook, so the own-entry correction path keeps working. Tested in test_rules.py [weights delete guard].

comments.author: Jan-Henrik Bruhn
comments.text: When redoing the weights rules, also make the boundary relations (animal/case/org) immutable on update — migration 1700000043 (federfall-621) deliberately skipped weights because they are org-wide writable today, so a re-point currently grants nothing extra; that changes once weights get scoped rules.

title: Security: OAuth2 provisioning trusts unverified email claim; first-signin-becomes-supervisor race
description:
oauth2_provisioning.pb.js:97-104 falls back to rawUser.email when no verified email is reported, then marks the account verified:true and emailVisibility:true. With an IdP allowing arbitrary unverified emails, this puts an attacker-chosen address into the org roster as verified. Separately, on a fresh instance the first OAuth2 sign-in becomes supervisor — a race worth documenting for operators who expose the server before bootstrapping.

Fix: only set verified when the provider actually verified the email; document the bootstrap race in the operator guide.
close_reason: oauth2_provisioning.pb.js only sets verified=true when the provider reported the email verified (ou.email); the rawUser.email fallback stays usable but unverified (roster shows invite-pending badge; guest wall keys off role so nobody is blocked). FEDERFALL_OIDC_TRUST_EMAIL=true overrides for vetted private IdPs. Bootstrap race documented in docs/DEPLOYMENT.md + backend README.

title: Security: geocoding proxy has no rate limiting (contradicts CLAUDE.md)
description:
CLAUDE.md describes the geocode proxy as providing 'server-side rate-limiting', but pb_hooks/geocode.pb.js has none. Any authenticated user can relay unlimited, unbounded-length queries to the upstream geocoder; against public OSM Nominatim this violates its 1 req/s policy and risks an instance-wide block. The cache softens repeats but not unique queries.

Fix: add PocketBase rate-limit rules for /api/federfall/geocode*; cap the q length.

title: Undo snackbar (countdown) for reversible destructive actions
description: Replace confirm dialogs on reversible deletes with a SnackBar that shows an Undo action and a small countdown timer; the action only commits after the grace window elapses.
design:
Pattern the user likes: when the user deletes a reversible item, show a SnackBar with an 'Undo' action + a small countdown indicator (e.g. ~5s shrinking progress / ticking number in the SnackBar content). The delete is DEFERRED — only sent to the backend when the window lapses without an undo.

IMPORTANT (correctness): defer the server delete rather than delete-then-recreate. PocketBase records carry ids, relations and cascade children (e.g. medications -> administrations, case_conditions, markings applied in a case); recreating on undo would mint a new id and orphan/duplicate relations. So: optimistically hide the row in the UI (e.g. a pending-delete set the list provider filters out), start the timer, and call repo.delete() only on expiry. On Undo: cancel the timer, drop the pending mark, restore the row. Guard against navigating away mid-window (commit on dispose, or cancel + keep).

Build a reusable helper in lib/ui/widgets, e.g. showUndoSnackBar(context, {message, onCommit, duration}) or an UndoAction wrapper, with the countdown widget (TweenAnimationBuilder over a LinearProgressIndicator/CircularProgressIndicator). l10n: an 'Undo' label + per-item '<thing> deleted' messages.

Candidate sites (currently AlertDialog confirm -> _delete): the case-timeline tiles — marking_tile, condition_entry_tile, weight_entry_tile, journal_entry_tile, placement_tile, exam_tile, follow_up_tile, medication_tiles (dose), quarantine_tile. Admin code-list deletes (conditions / marking types / admission reasons / medication routes) are also good fits. Keep an explicit confirm dialog for genuinely high-stakes / irreversible actions (e.g. member removal, disposition) — decide per action.

Roll out incrementally: land the helper + one or two tiles first, then convert the rest.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable undo-snackbar helper exists with a visible countdown. Deleting a converted item shows the snackbar; Undo within the window cancels the delete with NO backend call; letting it lapse commits the delete (and survives leaving the screen). Applied to at least the case-timeline delete actions. flutter analyze clean + flutter test green.

comments.text:
DESIGN (agreed 2026-06-25) — relational, follows case_conditions child pattern. Optional & skippable; intake never blocked. Repeatable timeline record (intake exam + later re-exams), one showExamSheet() + tile + sealed _Event.

Two collections:

exams (one row/exam):
  case (rel), animal (rel, denormalized like weights), examined_at (date, def now),
  examiner (rel user), body_condition (int 1-5; 1=emaciated 3=ideal 5=obese, nullable),
  hydration (enum: normal/mild/moderate/severe, nullable),
  mentation (enum: bright/quiet/depressed/unresponsive = BAR/QAR/.., nullable),
  notes (text), org.

exam_findings (child, one row per ASSESSED system — sparse):
  exam (rel), system (enum BodySystem), status (enum FindingStatus: normal/abnormal),
  note (text), org.
  Fetched in ONE query per case (filter exam.case=X), grouped client-side under each exam
  (same concurrent timeline-source pattern as worklist). No N+1.

Enums in enums.dart with wire values: Hydration, Mentation, BodySystem, FindingStatus
(body_condition stays plain int). Labels via cases_labels.dart, German primary.

UI sheet = 3 zones: (1) vitals always visible — BCS segmented 1-5, hydration & mentation
chips, nothing preselected; (2) "Befunde nach Körperregion" collapsed, each system o.B./
auffällig tri-state via untouched, note field appears only on auffällig; (3) free notes.
Intake entry = optional collapsed expander reusing same widget body; weight shown read-only
(stays its own kind/trend), not re-entered.

Rejected: JSON findings blob (untyped, unqueryable, breaks fromRecord/wire-enum convention);
fixed per-system columns (migration churn per system, mostly-null cols).

Defer to later: per-system numeric subscores (pododermatitis 1-5, 9-pt keel), photo on a
finding, cross-exam by-system reporting.

description: Optional MFA (encourage for supervisors).

title: Setup UX: warn/reject on explicit http:// server URL in server setup
description: server_probe.dart defaults schemeless input to https:// (good) but accepts an explicitly typed http:// URL, which would send the bearer token in cleartext. In practice the OS blocks it (no usesCleartextTraffic on Android, no ATS exceptions on iOS) so the user just gets an opaque network error. Fix: reject or warn on http:// in the setup UI for defense in depth and a clearer error message (dev/localhost may need an escape hatch for the development flavor which uses http://localhost:8090). Found in 2026-07-03 OWASP review.
acceptance_criteria: Typing an http:// server URL yields a clear warning/error instead of an opaque failure; dev flavor against localhost still works.
close_reason: Fixed in commits f4cdec6, 56a39f4, 813f45b, 0b34ceb, 8341830

title: Logging: add PII/token scrubbing funnel before any crash-reporting (Sentry) integration
description: app_logger.dart passes raw error objects to developer.log; ClientException/RepositoryException carry request URLs (incl. short-lived ?token= file params) and response bodies (validation errors echo submitted fields, e.g. finder PII). Today this only reaches local logcat/DevTools and prod is gated to info+ — low impact. But the logger comment invites a future Sentry/error-reporting hook at this funnel: DO NOT wire one without a scrubber stripping tokens, auth headers, and PII from URLs/bodies first (OWASP A09). Also operator-doc note (DEPLOYMENT.md): scrub 'token' query param from reverse-proxy access logs — PocketBase protected-file tokens travel as ?token= (~2min TTL). Found in 2026-07-03 OWASP review.
acceptance_criteria: Either a scrubbing layer exists at the app_logger funnel (tested for token/PII redaction), or a prominent comment blocks naive Sentry wiring; DEPLOYMENT.md mentions proxy log scrubbing of the token param.
close_reason: Fixed in commits f4cdec6, 56a39f4, 813f45b, 0b34ceb, 8341830

title: Security: add Referrer-Policy + Permissions-Policy headers to SPA responses
description: web_headers.pb.js sets CSP/nosniff/frame protections for the SPA but no Referrer-Policy or Permissions-Policy. Referrer leakage is limited (only map-tile origin is cross-origin) but cheap to close. Fix: add 'Referrer-Policy: same-origin' and a restrictive Permissions-Policy (camera/geolocation self as needed for intake photo + location features, rest denied). HSTS stays the reverse proxy's job (documented). Found in 2026-07-03 OWASP review.
acceptance_criteria: SPA responses carry both headers; web camera/location features still work; file responses unchanged (already default-src 'none'; sandbox).
close_reason: Fixed in commits f4cdec6, 56a39f4, 813f45b, 0b34ceb, 8341830

title: Finder retention keys on client-writable disposed_at instead of server created date
description: finder_retention.pb.js:68-105 (caseEndInfo) treats a case as ended via status == 'disposed' and anchors the retention window on the latest disposition's disposed_at (fallback created, then case updated). disposed_at is client-writable, so a carer with edit access could backdate it to accelerate PII scrubbing of a finder, or an incorrect disposed_at silently shifts when GDPR anonymisation fires. Low impact (only affects WHEN PII is deleted, org-internal), but retention is a compliance feature. Fix: anchor the window on the disposition's server-side created autodate rather than user-supplied disposed_at.
close_reason: Fixed in commits f4cdec6, 56a39f4, 813f45b, 0b34ceb, 8341830

title: Case-number year uses UTC admitted_at (consider org-local timezone)
description: Follow-up from federfall-4k4. The year prefix comes from the UTC admitted_at, so a case admitted just after midnight Jan 1 CET gets the previous year's number — consider an org-local timezone (org.settings) when deriving the year. (The second half of the original issue — global unique index vs per-org generation — was fixed in 1700000046_case_number_org_unique.js.)
close_reason: won't fix — a UTC boundary case at midnight on Jan 1 CET, not worth the org-local-timezone complexity

title: tests/run.sh: tempdir cleanup fails on root-owned upload storage
description: run.sh's cleanup trap does rm -rf $DATA on the host, but pb_data/storage files created by the container (root) survive: 'rm: cannot remove .../storage/...png: Permission denied'. Leaves litter in /tmp after every suite run that exercises file uploads. Fix: do the rm inside a container (docker run --rm -v $DATA:/pb/pb_data busybox rm -rf /pb/pb_data/storage) or run the PB container with --user $(id -u).
close_reason: cleanup trap now clears root-owned container files via a throwaway container run before rm -rf on the host; verified with a live run.sh — 224/224 tests pass, no tempdir left behind

title: Rewrite repository layer to use $PocketBase RequestPolicy.cacheAndNetwork
description:
All ~20 repository classes in packages/federfall_data/lib/src/repositories/ need to be reimplemented on top of $PocketBase instead of PbRepository.

Pattern for each repository:
- Keep Repository<T> interface unchanged
- Implement methods using $PocketBase.collection(name).{getOne,getList,create,update,delete}()
- Use requestPolicy: RequestPolicy.cacheAndNetwork (the offline-first default)
- Convert RecordModel → domain model via existing Case.fromRecord / Animal.fromRecord etc.
- Drop PrefsRecordCache usage entirely

Priority order: cases, animals, case_conditions, treatments, weights (the core daily-use data) first; then worklist_entries, findings, exams, and remaining collections.

title: Export PocketBase schema and add pb_schema.json as a bundled Flutter asset
description:
pocketbase_drift requires the collection schema to be exported from PocketBase Admin UI (Settings → Export collections → JSON) and bundled as a Flutter asset.

- Export the current schema from the running PocketBase instance
- Save as apps/federfall/assets/pb_schema.json
- Add assets/pb_schema.json to flutter: assets: in pubspec.yaml
- Wire into app bootstrap: await client.db.setSchema(schema)
- Add schema re-export step to the migration/runbook docs

NOTE: The schema must be re-exported after every PocketBase collection migration. Document this as part of the deployment runbook.

title: Add pocketbase_drift to pubspec.yaml and wire $PocketBase in pocketbase_provider
description:
Add pocketbase_drift ^0.4.3 as a dependency in apps/federfall/pubspec.yaml.

In apps/federfall/lib/core/pocketbase/pocketbase_provider.dart:
- Replace (or parallel) PocketBase() instantiation with $PocketBase.database(url)
- Wire $AuthStore.prefs() for auth persistence (web: localStorage, native: Keychain/Keystore via drift)
- Configure default RequestPolicy (cacheAndNetwork) on the client
- Export pb_schema.json as a bundled asset in pubspec.yaml assets section

Keep the official PocketBase SDK available alongside for operations requiring precise real-time behaviour.

title: Add background sync via WorkManager (iOS/Android) — push writes when app is backgrounded
description:
pocketbase_drift's sync retry only fires when the app is active and connectivity returns. For wildlife rehabilitators who log a medication and immediately put the phone in their pocket, the write queues locally but only syncs when they re-open the app.

- Evaluate adding workmanager (^3.0.0) to trigger sync on backgrounded iOS/Android
- On connectivity RESTORED event, schedule a WorkManager one-off task that calls _retrySyncForAllServices()
- On web: use ServiceWorker background sync API (navigator.serviceWorker.ready.then(...))
- Add retryLocal() progress reporting to the UI (sync indicator in app bar)
- Test: kill app immediately after creating a case offline → app restart should sync it

title: Handle logout — set clearOnLogout: false on $AuthStore; verify schema persistence
description:
pocketbase_drift's $AuthStore.clear() wipes the entire local Drift DB by default. For Federfall, the cache should be preserved on logout (carers may share a device), only cleared on explicit app reset.

- Set clearOnLogout: false on $AuthStore
- Verify schema (pb_schema.json) is NOT cleared on logout
- Verify case/case_condition/treatment data IS cleared on logout
- Test: log in → create a case offline → log out → log back in → case should be gone
- Remove SecureAuthTokenStorage / PrefsAuthTokenStorage once $AuthStore.prefs is wired

title: Adapt LiveRefresh / case_realtime.dart for pocketbase_drift's realtime bridge
description:
pocketbase_drift pipes PocketBase realtime events into the local Drift DB (onEvent() → $create / $update / $delete on local SQLite). Federfall's existing ServiceMixin, LiveRefresh and case_realtime.dart wiring should be checked for compatibility.

- Verify ServiceMixin's watchRecords() stream still fires and triggers UI rebuilds
- Verify onEvent() writes to Drift do not interfere with existing stream logic
- Adapt caseRealtime to use $PocketBase.realtime if needed
- Test: create/update/delete a case from Admin UI → verify Flutter app updates within 5s

Note: the realtime service is currently delegated to the base PocketBase SDK class (not overridden in $PocketBase).

title: Set up WASM SQLite for web (sqlite3.wasm + drift_worker.dart.js)
description:
pocketbase_drift uses Drift with SQLite. On web this requires WASM files served from the web/ directory.

- Download sqlite3.wasm from the sqlite3.dart releases
- Download drift_worker.dart.js from the drift package
- Place both in apps/federfall/web/
- Verify web build succeeds (flutter build web)
- Test offline reads/writes in browser (Chrome DevTools → Network → Offline)

Currently Federfall uses SharedPreferences (localStorage) for web cache — this replaces it with a proper SQLite store.

title: Migrate to pocketbase_drift — offline-first sync engine
description:
Replace the current read-through cache (PrefsRecordCache) with pocketbase_drift (^0.4.3), a proper offline-first SQLite-backed layer for PocketBase.

CURRENT BEHAVIOUR (broken):
- All writes (create/update/delete) throw RepositoryException.network immediately when offline
- Cache is bounded: 100 records / 20 lists per collection, no TTL, FIFO eviction
- offlineBanner shows "Offline – showing saved data" — misleading, because no writes are possible
- No background sync; writes never survive app restart

DESIRED BEHAVIOUR:
- Writes queue locally in SQLite when offline; sync automatically on reconnect
- Reads served from cache instantly; network updates in background
- Conflict resolution: 5 retries then silent discard (acceptable for wildlife rehab use case)
- Schema-bundled validation for local writes

See: https://pub.dev/packages/pocketbase_drift
See: internal code review — pocketbase_drift gap analysis

title: Deprecate and remove legacy PrefsRecordCache, PbRepository and repository_providers
description:
Once all repositories are migrated (offline-04), remove the legacy offline infrastructure:

- Delete packages/federfall_data/lib/src/pb_repository.dart
- Delete apps/federfall/lib/data/cache/prefs_record_cache.dart
- Delete apps/federfall/lib/data/repository_providers.dart (replace with new provider layout)
- Remove cache fields from PbCasesRepository, PbAnimalsRepository, etc.
- Remove SharedPreferences dependency from pubspec.yaml if no longer needed (except for app settings)
- Update tests that mock PbRepository or PrefsRecordCache
- Update README / offline architecture docs

title: Update offline banner UX — reflect actual sync queue state
description:
The current offline banner reads "Offline – showing saved data." This implies writes work offline, but they don't with the current cache.

After migration to pocketbase_drift, the banner should reflect actual behaviour:
- OFFLINE + pending local writes: "Offline — changes saved locally, syncing when online"
- OFFLINE, no pending writes: "Offline — showing cached data"
- ONLINE + pending writes: hide banner or show subtle sync indicator
- ONLINE, no pending writes: hide banner

Update l10n.offlineBanner and related strings. Wire sync status from $PocketBase.retryLocal() progress events.
